races.race_name: Racing TV Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Surprised
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who has dropped to 6f for last two starts; gradually back to form this season for new stable, close third over C&D (good to soft) most recently; possibilities off same mark.
runners.comment: Gradually back to form this season for new stable, close third over C&D most recently

runners.name: Stirrup Cup
runners.spotlight: Shaped well and looked rather unlucky in two races at Thirsk in April; two attempts over this C&D (soft) comprise a second-place finish and success; likely player if bouncing back from below-par effort last time.
runners.comment: Has an encouraging C&D record; likely player if bouncing back from below-par run last time

runners.name: Rich Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Has shown most of his handicap form over 7f, winning twice on AW; however, both 2026 efforts suggest this return to 6f is worth exploring; effective on turf and he's on a handy mark.
runners.comment: Both 2026 efforts suggest this return to 6f is worth exploring; on a handy mark

runners.name: Macedonian
runners.spotlight: AW scorer for previous yard; inconsistent for current stable and has failed to show his form in two starts this term; well treated on best turf efforts but he's not particularly solid.
runners.comment: Inconsistent for current stable; yet to win on turf

runners.name: Coconut Bay
runners.spotlight: Has done most of her winning at 7f; ran respectably in last 6f attempt but was disappointing at Catterick (usually one of her favoured tracks) most recently and needs to rebound.
runners.comment: Most wins over 7f; disappointing at Catterick last time

runners.name: Too Much
runners.spotlight: Has done her winning on AW but ran well in last two turf attempts; however, brings a doubt over current form, having performed poorly in sole outing this year; upset in stalls and withdrawn last month (28-1 at the time).
runners.comment: All wins on AW; ran poorly in sole outing this year

runners.name: Baba Reza
runners.spotlight: Has had wind surgery on two occasions, namely prior to reappearance success in 2024 (off 3lb higher) and prior to this 2026 seasonal debut; today may prove the time to catch him.
runners.comment: Bids to win on reappearance, after wind surgery, for a second time; interesting

runners.name: Harry's Halo
runners.spotlight: Has shown most of his form over 6f on ground softer than good; close third in big field over 5f at Doncaster in sole run this term; enters calculations off same mark back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has shown most of his form over 6f; close third over 5f in sole run this term
runners.owner: Jones, Keane, Kryswicki & Kf Racing Club

runners.name: Pop Star
runners.spotlight: Ended a run of duck eggs with 5f success at Carlisle (good) ten days ago and although back up 5lb he remains well treated on historical data, which includes 6f win off current mark (two years ago); respected.
runners.comment: Scored over 5f at Carlisle ten days ago; recorded a 6f win off current mark two years ago

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Deputy Vice
runners.spotlight: £145,000 breeze-up 2yo; had a good draw at Chester (6f, good; 11-1) and held his own in what looked a useful novice race; that sets the standard.
runners.comment: Held his own in what looked a useful novice at Chester; sets the standard

runners.name: Light Of Dawn
runners.spotlight: Made 140,000gns as a yearling and 625,000euros at a breeze-up sale; half-sister to ten winners including some some smart sprinters, while the dam was a 5f 2yo Listed winner; of major interest for several standout reasons.
runners.comment: Made 140,000gns as a yearling and 625,000euros at a breeze-up sale; well related

runners.name: Capilano
runners.spotlight: Gelded 72,000gns yearling; inexperience was evident when a rallying third at Newcastle, finishing ahead of Caeruleus; improvement on the cards.
runners.comment: 72,000gns yearling; inexperience was evident when a rallying third at Newcastle

runners.name: Caeruleus
runners.spotlight: 80,000gns yearling; had only just turned two when fourth at Newcastle 20 days ago; has 2l to find with Capilano but this son of Blue Point was getting the hang of things late on.
runners.comment: This son of Blue Point was getting the hang of things late on at Newcastle

races.race_name: Carlisle Racecourse Supporting IJF Beneficiaries Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Always Blue
runners.spotlight: One of the main form contenders, having finished second at Ripon (1m2f) then just over 1l behind Pearl when 50-1 fifth at York (1m); however, unlikely to reverse placings with that filly, who has bigger potential.
runners.comment: One of the main form players but is unlikely to reverse York placings with Pearl

runners.name: Pearl
runners.spotlight: Sister to French 1,000 Guineas/French Oaks winner Avenir Certain; easy to back but showed clear promise with 1l third in novice event at York (1m, good) where she was the sole newcomer (belated debut) in a field of seven; this 4yo filly is the form pick and open to progress to boot.
runners.comment: Nicely bred; showed promise with 1l third at York; the form pick and open to progress

runners.name: Midnight Serenade
runners.spotlight: Twilight Son half-sister to winners Earn Your Stripes (5f/6f; RPR 59) and Willy Il Coyote (Italian 6.7f-1m); dam 1m4f AW scorer (58); yard not associated with winning newcomers.
runners.comment: Yard not associated with winning newcomers

runners.name: Foxy Night
runners.spotlight: Bred to stay at least 1m; belied her 50-1 odds with fourth of ten, best of the newcomers, in 7f AW contest at Kempton; a possible danger, provided she builds on that effort.
runners.comment: Possible danger, provided she builds on Kempton AW effort (50-1 fourth)

runners.name: Stolen Kisses
runners.spotlight: Sister to Italian 1m Flat/2m jumps winner Cult Figure, half-sister to 1m1f winner Givemehoperosa (RPR 73); dam 1m-9.4f scorer (100); pedigree doesn't set the pulse racing; belated debut aged four.
runners.comment: Pedigree doesn't set the pulse racing; makes belated debut

runners.name: Marhayb
runners.spotlight: Finished 2l fifth of ten in 7f AW event at Newcastle where she started second favourite behind a gelding in the same ownership; recorded an ordinary RPR but may well improve with the experience under her belt.
runners.comment: May improve with debut experience (2l fifth on AW) under her belt

runners.name: Fair Isle Sioux
runners.spotlight: 6yo mare who has been absent for over two years since her sole start, namely keeping-on fifth of seven in Redcar contest (6f, soft); likely best watched.
runners.comment: 6yo mare who has been absent for over two years since her sole start

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV Live Handicap

runners.name: Lanarra
runners.spotlight: Improved form since racing in 6f handicaps, recording 66-1 win on AW last autumn then 33-1 second at Catterick (good) on reappearance; may well remain competitive.
runners.comment: Improved form (won/second) since racing in 6f handicaps; may remain competitive

runners.name: Battenburg Belle
runners.spotlight: Maiden who ran well at Ripon (good) and Catterick (good to firm) last month, taking her record in 6f handicaps to 322; threatening to win a race of this nature.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 322 in 6f handicaps; threatening to win a race of this nature

runners.name: Auspicious
runners.spotlight: Unraced on ground softer than good; ran well (neck second) over C&D ten days ago when bidding to follow up his narrow success at Redcar; remains in top form.
runners.comment: Good second over C&D ten days ago when bidding to follow up his Redcar win

runners.name: Mereside Spark
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; proved somewhat resurgent with fourth of six upped to 6f at Catterick (good to firm; bled from nose) most recently; may build on that effort.
runners.comment: Proved somewhat resurgent last time and may build on that effort

runners.name: Realistic Dream
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden whose lack of consistency has continued for new stable; likely best watched unless the market suggests a big run is expected.
runners.comment: Lack of consistency has continued for new stable

runners.name: Truly Special
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has weak claims on her 1m/7f efforts this term for new yard but, judged on pedigree, may improve for this return to sprinting (second attempt).
runners.comment: Pedigree suggests she may improve for this return to sprinting

runners.name: Breeze Star
runners.spotlight: Broadly regressive maiden whose chance depends on the effects of physical tweak (wind surgery since last run).
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of wind surgery

runners.name: The Cyprus Pearl
runners.spotlight: Turf/handicap debutante who is far from solid on her AW form; the new scenario needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: New scenario needs to make a big difference

runners.name: Independent Angel
runners.spotlight: Has achieved little in terms of form and two duck eggs this season highlight her poor chance.
runners.comment: Has achieved little in terms of form

runners.name: Evelyn's Garden
runners.spotlight: Has recorded RPRs in the 30s on Newcastle AW; change of venue/surface needs to prompt a good deal of improvement.
runners.comment: Change of venue needs to prompt big improvement

runners.name: Charcon
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on form mostly shown on Newcastle AW; failed to beat a rival in seller in sole turf attempt (debut).
runners.comment: Far from solid on form

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Epidavros
runners.spotlight: Losing run is mounting up and her three fourth placings this season reflect stagnation, rather than building towards a win; that said, she has won over C&D (soft) and returns here off a workable mark.
runners.comment: Three fourth placings this term; has won over C&D and returns here off a workable mark

runners.name: Blufferonthebus
runners.spotlight: In reasonably good form, having made the frame twice last month, third at Catterick latest; dual winner at Carlisle (soft/good to firm) and returns to this venue off a favourable mark; respected.
runners.comment: In reasonably good form and she's a dual winner at Carlisle; respected

runners.name: Ravishing Beauty
runners.spotlight: Solid spell of form last term featured two wins and three second-place finishes; has gone rather off the boil since but a revival would give her good claims off current mark.
runners.comment: Has gone rather off the boil since solid spell last term

runners.name: Angela's Baar
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has failed to build on debut promise; however, made some late headway upped to 1m last time and there may yet be a bigger effort in her in handicaps.
runners.comment: May yet have a bigger effort in her in handicaps

runners.name: We've Got This
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-13 and her form has dipped a touch in two outings this term; normally a consistent sort otherwise and, as per many times, has frame possibilities if back on song.
runners.comment: Form has dipped a touch this term; normally a consistent sort otherwise

runners.name: Music Academy
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who posted best effort when third at Kempton (AW) on penultimate start; may be capable of progress now handicapping, provided she takes to first-time headgear; shortlisted.
runners.comment: May be capable of progress now handicapping, provided headgear works well

runners.name: Positive Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Has gone rather backwards since a good autumn effort on AW, disappointing favourite on handicap debut (7f) most recently; chance depends on the effects of new trip and first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of new trip and first-time headgear

runners.name: Emery Down
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-11 in handicaps since AW maiden win; however, steadily heading back in the right direction for new yard, fourth at Yarmouth (1m) most recently; possibilities off reduced mark.
runners.comment: Steadily heading back in the right direction for new yard; possibilities

runners.name: Rosemary May
runners.spotlight: Far from convincing on her handicap form since winning Ayr maiden in April 2025; best watched unless the betting suggests a turnaround is likely.
runners.comment: Far from convincing on her handicap form since maiden win

races.race_name: Follow Racing TV On X Handicap

runners.name: Redarna
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has been a prolific winner down the years, including at Carlisle; not solid on 2026 form but this drop back in grade may have positive effect; successful in last Class 5 attempt.
runners.comment: Drop back in grade may have positive effect; successful in last Class 5 attempt

runners.name: Runninsonofagun
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in most starts last term for new yard but form has dipped in two outings this term, latest over C&D; doesn't look as if he's about to belatedly add to his Irish 2yo win.
runners.comment: Form has dipped in two outings this term, latest over C&D

runners.name: Yermanthere
runners.spotlight: Form at Carlisle features a success in 2024 and close second last June, both off much higher marks; a possible danger provided he takes a sufficient step forward from Beverley reappearance duck egg.
runners.comment: Form at Carlisle features a success in 2024 and close second last June, off higher marks

runners.name: Krissy
runners.spotlight: Progressive since November, particularly on good ground this spring; gained most recent win over C&D; best to forgive latest effort (stumbled start, unable to adopt usual front-running tactics); remains of interest.
runners.comment: Best to forgive latest effort; progressive otherwise since November and is a C&D scorer

runners.name: Amancio
runners.spotlight: Has a record of 3-7 (Flat/hurdles) for current yard; creditable third at Newbury (1m4f, good) on seasonal debut most recently; major player off same mark, assuming he copes with this drop back in trip (stiff track and forecast slow ground are helpful in that respect).
runners.comment: Record of 3-7 (Flat/hurdles) for current yard; ran creditably at Newbury on reappearance

runners.name: King Of Fury
runners.spotlight: Triple AW winner who failed to get home, having raced too freely, over 1m2f in that sphere last time (still ran respectably); 0-12 on turf but was a good second off 6lb higher in last attempt; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 0-12 on turf but was a good second off 6lb higher in last attempt; not ruled out
runners.owner: Mrs K S Pratt & M Dods

runners.name: Star Cast
runners.spotlight: Generally progressive on AW/turf this year, winning four times over 1m2f; creditable third over 1m4f at Musselburgh last time; remains in form and may still have more to offer at this level; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Generally progressive this year; remains in form and may still have more to offer

runners.name: Sound Janet
runners.spotlight: Runner-up a few times for previous yard; folded tamely and finished last on stable/seasonal debut but her subsequent wind surgery may prompt a better effort.
runners.comment: Folded tamely and finished last on stable/seasonal debut; had wind surgery since

runners.name: Concert Boy
runners.spotlight: Does most of his racing at Newcastle (AW) where he has registered all five wins; generally consistent this year but seems to have a difficult task back on turf, being 8lb higher than last attempt.
runners.comment: Does most of his racing on Newcastle AW; 8lb higher than last turf attempt
runners.owner: Making Headway Racing And K Scott

runners.name: Pink Azalea
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly whose handicap record is split into two halves, namely a progressive one followed by a dismal one; however, 1-1 at this track and could revive if her favoured soft ground materialises.
runners.comment: 1-1 at this track and could revive if her favoured soft ground materialises

runners.name: Skinny Malinka
runners.spotlight: Absent since pulled up in December 2024 when making hurdles/stable debut; consistent six-race maiden on Flat for previous yard; market instructive.
runners.comment: Maiden; absent since 2024; market instructive

runners.name: Titainium
runners.spotlight: Absent since a dismal effort on soft ground last July and again has a doubt over the forecast surface; has gained both turf wins on good to firm.
runners.comment: Forecast ground isn't in his favour; has gained both turf wins on good to firm

runners.name: Cosmos Raj
runners.spotlight: Gained most recent success off 3lb higher last July, enhancing his good record at Ripon; not in anything like the same form since; difficult to support with confidence.
runners.comment: Gained most recent success last July; not in anything like the same form since

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now Handicap

runners.name: Albeyours
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who has form figures of 1253 since wearing cheekpieces, most recently third in big field at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) on seasonal debut; solid claims in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 1253 since wearing cheekpieces; solid claims

runners.name: Jkr Cobbler
runners.spotlight: Has gained most wins at Ayr/Newcastle but he defied a higher mark over C&D two years ago; basically running okay this year until never dangerous last time; has the ability to take a serious hand.
runners.comment: Defied a higher mark over C&D two years ago; capable of taking a serious hand

runners.name: Rwenearlytheredad
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery prior to Ayr reappearance effort that seemed to be needed; gained most recent success off 4lb higher over C&D (good to soft) just over a year ago and looks interesting back here.
runners.comment: Gained last success off 4lb higher over C&D; interesting back here

runners.name: Baby Rover
runners.spotlight: Registered sole win over 6f but recent efforts suggest she may need further now and she is less exposed over 7f than shorter; otherwise needs to step up on 2026 bare figures.
runners.comment: Less exposed over 7f than shorter but needs to improve on 2026 form
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Ideal Guest
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-13 for current stable but he remains 10lb below last winning mark and holds a good chance provided he builds on Catterick reappearance effort (finished third).
runners.comment: 0-13 for current stable but has a good chance if building on reappearance effort

runners.name: Dolly's Delight
runners.spotlight: Registered sole success on fast ground; has rather inconsistent form for current yard; brings doubts over the forecast surface and whether she'll be in top form.
runners.comment: Inconsistent form for current yard; gained sole win on good to firm
runners.owner: The Good Racing Company Ltd & Partner

runners.name: Tarlac
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 6f; inconsistent since sole win; his chance of taking advantage of a reduced mark partly depends on the effects of this return to 7f; record over this trip isn't fully conclusive.
runners.comment: Campaigned mostly over 6f; inconsistent since sole win
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Orbital Chime
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate; eventually lost his form during spring spell with James Owen and was sold for 2,200gns since last run; best watched unless the market suggests a revival is likely.
runners.comment: Needs a turnaround in his form; debut for new yard

runners.name: Pallas Lord
runners.spotlight: Has recorded the majority of his wins on Newcastle AW; below par over C&D ten days ago, failing to build on his Hamilton effort; still hasn't scored on turf since 2022.
runners.comment: Below par over C&D ten days ago; hasn't won on turf since 2022

runners.name: Sir David
runners.spotlight: Far from convincing on his efforts this term for new yard, latest back at Carlisle, but looks a plausible danger provided he revives; defied a 7lb higher mark over C&D last August (sole win).
runners.comment: Defied a 7lb higher mark over C&D last August (sole win); a danger if reviving

races.race_name: Heather Small Live @LeicesterRaces Saturday 4th July Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Bradbury
runners.spotlight: Much improved for handicaps but has thrown away his last two as he looked sure to win before hanging violently in the closing stages (1m2f); still went close on both occasions and the new eyeshield could be a significant addition.
runners.comment: Eyeshield could be a significant addition having thrown away his last two races

runners.name: Adores
runners.spotlight: Disappointed at Windsor last time (1m3f) but her fourth on handicap debut (1m AW) has worked out fine and perhaps 1m2f will suit.
runners.comment: Last run over 1m3f was disappointing; previous effort at 1m has worked out fine

runners.name: Heart Sign
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in a 7f AW maiden but hasn't kicked on at all in handicaps (1m-1m2f); ran okay on second run when once tried in a hood and that aid returns combined with blinkers.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in a 7f AW maiden but hasn't kicked on at all in handicaps

runners.name: Too Darn Spicy
runners.spotlight: 0-5 and no closer than 8l, with that best run coming on her debut; beaten about 10l and 12l in two handicaps, over 7f on good to firm and 1m on soft.
runners.comment: Beaten about 10l and 12l in two handicaps, over 7f on good to firm and 1m on soft

runners.name: Gwen's Girl
runners.spotlight: Easily her best run was when second on debut (6f, soft) and has finished well behind in her two handicaps (1m); the cheekpieces need to make a difference.
runners.comment: Well behind in her two handicaps (1m); the cheekpieces need to make a difference
runners.owner: E Holmes, M Graham And P Day

runners.name: Captain Cairney
runners.spotlight: Had eight runs at two, showing best form when first and second in 1m2f handicaps at Chelmsford; bit to prove on turf but might progress further after being gelded.
runners.comment: First and second in 1m2f handicaps on the AW last season; has been gelded
runners.owner: J O'Mulloy And W R Muir

runners.name: Sahm Naif
runners.spotlight: Beaten between 12l and 18l in his qualifying races (7f/1m), the latest on stable debut after being gelded; may show more in handicaps but hard to be confident.
runners.comment: Beaten between 12l and 18l in his qualifying races (7f/1m); hard to be confident

races.race_name: Go West Live @LeicesterRaces Saturday 4th July Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB/IRE) (Div I)

runners.name: Kach Above
runners.spotlight: Well-bred colt who made a promising start when fourth at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last month; only seventh of 12 at Windsor last time but he wasn't beaten far and that was a step forward from his initial effort; sets the standard on that form and he's open to more progress on this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Two promising efforts over 6f and and he sets the standard at this new trip; key player

runners.name: Better Nature
runners.spotlight: Never involved when 20-1 on Windsor debut (6f, good) last month but that was behind a useful prospect and he was only beaten around 5l; wasn't far behind Kach Above in that race and he's a possible improver upped in trip on his second start.
runners.comment: Showed some ability on Windsor debut and he's not ruled out upped in trip

runners.name: Farndale
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and was always behind when sixth of seven on Redcar debut (7f, good) three weeks ago; out of a middle-distance winner and she's probably one for longer trips later on.
runners.comment: Out of a middle-distance winner and she made a low-key start at Redcar (7f) last month

runners.name: See Amna
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; sister to French 7.5f winner Zelkova, half-sister to Italian 7.5f-1m1f winner Alroucha; dam French 1m1f/9.5f winner (including Group 3; RPR 105), out of 1m2f winning half-sister to useful 1m-1m4f winner Stepping Ahead; yard 19% with 2yos this season and she's an interesting newcomer in this ordinary race.
runners.comment: Yard 19% with 2yos this season and she's an interesting newcomer in this ordinary race

runners.name: Mrs Mole
runners.spotlight: April foal; 12,000gns yearling; second foal; dam Listed-placed 7f AW winner (RPR 98), half-sister to 1m6f winner Tristram, out of maiden half-sister to 2m Listed winner King Of Wands; first 2yo runner for the yard this season and she's probably best watched.
runners.comment: First 2yo runner for the yard this season and she's probably best watched

runners.name: Wells Fargos Envoy
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam 6f-1m4f winner (including 2yo; RPR 97), half-sister to 7.5f/1m winner Bob, out of maiden half-sister to 5f-7f winner Crystal Moments; one of two runners for yard and market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: One of two runners for yard and market should guide on debut

runners.name: Parioca
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns daughter of Cotai Glory; beaten less than 3l when fifth of six at Southwell (7f; cheekpieces applied) last month but she didn't achieve much in that weak race; blinkers are now tried and she needs a major step forward on this switch to turf.
runners.comment: Didn't achieve much on Southwell debut and she needs improvement with blinkers now tried

runners.name: Waiting For Archie
runners.spotlight: Well-held fifth of eight at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) last month, recording an RPR of only 43; this step up in trip could suit on pedigree but he needs major improvement on his second start.
runners.comment: Well held on Thirsk debut and he needs major improvement upped in trip

runners.name: Pertinent
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and finished a remote last of ten on her debut here (5f, good to firm) last month; this trip looks a better fit on pedigree but she needs a transformation on her second start.
runners.comment: 66-1 and finished a remote last of ten on her debut here (5f, good to firm) last month

runners.name: Lord Ragnar
runners.spotlight: 11,000euros yearling whose dam won over 12.6f; didn't show much when 33-1 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month and he's probably a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Didn't show much when 33-1 at Windsor and he's probably a longer-term prospect
runners.owner: Sarah Fern, Patricia James And Partner

races.race_name: Go West Live @LeicesterRaces Saturday 4th July Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (GBB/IRE) (Div II)

runners.name: Mottaret
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of stamina on dam's side and made an encouraging debut when fourth of seven at Redcar (7f, good; 28-1) three weeks ago; that form puts him firmly in the picture and he should be more streetwise this time; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Showed promise at Redcar (7f) and that form puts him firmly in the picture; shortlisted

runners.name: Celtic Charioteer
runners.spotlight: January foal; brother to UAE 9.5f dirt winner Withering (RPR 109); never involved after a slow start on Windsor debut (6f, good) last month but he made some late gains and was only beaten around 5l; should know more this time and he's a likely improver upped in trip.
runners.comment: Well-bred colt who shaped with promise on his Windsor debut; in the mix upped in trip

runners.name: Hoosthat
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam 1m4f AW winner (RPR 64), out of close relative of US 8.5f stakes winner Bobcat Greeley, is granddaughter of half-sister to US 1m4f Grade 1 winner Silver Supreme and to dam of Miesque; yard 3-50 with 2yos last season and he could be a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Yard 3-50 with 2yos last season and he could be a longer-term prospect

runners.name: Jazzy Bay
runners.spotlight: Made encouraging debut when sixth of 12 at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) last month and he built on that when a close third over C&D (good to firm) five days later; that was a small-field seller last time but he has leading claims on that form and yard is 19% with 2yos this season.
runners.comment: Two promising efforts last month and latest was over C&D; key player for in-form yard

runners.name: Tiora Time
runners.spotlight: 16,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to 6f AW winner Tantomile (RPR 55); dam 7f-1m3f winner (84), half-sister to winners Afta Party (1m Flat/2m1f hurdle) and Line House (7f including 2yo), out of half-sister to 1m6f Group 2 winner Franklins Gardens; has speed and stamina in pedigree and market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: Has speed and stamina in pedigree and market should guide on debut

runners.name: She Is Mighty
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 74), half-sister to winners Educate (smart 1m-10.4f), Hippolytus (1m2f) and Ban Shoof (9.4f-1m4f), out of 7.4f-1m4f winning half-sister to 6f/7f Group 1 winner Somnus; has a striking pedigree and she needs checking in market on debut.
runners.comment: Has a striking pedigree and she needs checking in market on debut

runners.name: Mojito Madness
runners.spotlight: April foal; 8,500euros yearling, resold 16,000gns yearling; brother to winners Swing Out Sister (5f 2yo; RPR 76) and Seahorse Syd (1m/1m2f including 2yo; 65), half-brother to four winners including Online Alexander (5f including 2yo; 105) and Bnedel (1m AW 2yo/10.4f; 87); dam 7f winner (70); plenty to like on paper and he needs watching in market on debut.
runners.comment: Related to plenty of of winners and he needs watching in market on debut

runners.name: Jazzy Blue
runners.spotlight: Ran green and she finished a remote sixth of eight on Yarmouth debut (6f, good to soft; 18-1) last month; should benefit from that experience but she needs major improvement upped in trip.
runners.comment: Ran green and she finished a remote sixth of eight on Yarmouth debut (6f, good to soft)

runners.name: Tiger In The Tree
runners.spotlight: Dam once-raced (RPR 58) half-sister to a hurdle winner; didn't show much at a big price on Redcar debut (6f, good) last month and she's probably one for later on.
runners.comment: Didn't show much at a big price on Redcar debut and she's probably one for later on

runners.name: Allaboutthelunch
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival on his debut here (5f, good to firm) in April and was out the back again at another big price at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last month.
runners.comment: 40-1 in two runs over sprint trips and he's finished out the back on both occasions

races.race_name: Racing Again This Saturday Evening 13th June Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Vichenza
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to 6f/7f winner Zoum Zoum (including Listed; RPR 113); looked inexperienced and had some traffic issues on Salisbury debut (6f, good) last month but she kept on well to finish third of 12 behind the favourite; that form puts her firmly in the picture and she should know more this time; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Showed plenty of promise when third on Salisbury debut last month; interesting contender

runners.name: Heddon Street
runners.spotlight: 32,000euros half-brother to nine winners including My Lea (Italian 5f/6f including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 106) and Royal Lea (Italian 5.5f/6f including Listed; 94); 11-2 on Yarmouth debut (6f, good) last October and he came from off the pace to win in emphatic style; has been gelded since but he's strongly respected under a penalty on his return; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Easy win in sole 2yo run and he's respected under a penalty on his return

runners.name: Jellystone Park
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth on Lingfield debut in March (6f, AW) and he built on that when a front-running second at Doncaster (6f, good; beaten a nose) last month; needs to take another step forward here but it's still early days and he's in the mix with hood added; bolted before the start and withdrawn last Monday (5-2 at the time).
runners.comment: Went very close at Doncaster last month and he's open to more progress; in the mix

runners.name: Muchacho
runners.spotlight: 12,000euros foal, £16,000 yearling, 11,000euros 2yo; third foal; closely related to 5f AW winner Love Rock (RPR 59), half-brother to 5.7f winner Plutonic (71); dam 5f 2yo winner (including Listed; 94), half-sister to useful 5f/6f winner Gordon Bennett; has plenty of speed in his pedigree but this looks a tough starting point.
runners.comment: Has plenty of speed in his pedigree but this looks a tough starting point

races.race_name: DSK Environmental Handicap

runners.name: Percy's Star
runners.spotlight: Finished last of the four at Windsor two weeks ago but he looks well-in for handicap debut on his previous second there (6f, good to firm), and cheekpieces are now added.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces added for handicap debut; definite chance on his penultimate effort

runners.name: Love Alive
runners.spotlight: The most exposed of these after nine runs and the win came on the AW (5f; off 2lb higher); only sixth of eight on recent stable debut at Redcar.
runners.comment: The most exposed of these after nine runs; only sixth on recent stable debut at Redcar

runners.name: Life After Love
runners.spotlight: All three wins at Southwell but seemed fine on turf debut when beaten a little over 2l at Lingfield (good); down 1lb; can run well.
runners.comment: Three AW wins; seemed fine on turf debut when beaten a little over 2l at Lingfield

runners.name: I'm Dan Dare
runners.spotlight: 0-6 for Adrian Nicholls; was backed on recent stable debut at Catterick (5f, good to firm) and he was never headed in winning well; that was a Class 6 and he's gone up 8lb, so has more on his plate; still much respected.
runners.comment: Made all on stable debut; that was a Class 6 and he's up 8lb, so has more on his plate

runners.name: Bessie Wallis
runners.spotlight: Won a weak AW novice last season; no further improvement this term and behind Life After Love last time; the hood needs to bring her on.
runners.comment: Behind Life After Love last time; the hood needs to bring her on

runners.name: Nebbia
runners.spotlight: Unplaced on handicap/seasonal debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) but she wasn't beaten far into fifth and she shaped as though the run would bring her on; capable of better.
runners.comment: Unplaced on handicap/seasonal debut at Windsor (6f, good/firm) but she wasn't beaten far

runners.name: Enter Sandman
runners.spotlight: Has done well on the AW since fitted with a tongue-tie and cheekpieces, coming away with the favourite last time; raised another 4lb so needs to transfer the improvement to turf.
runners.comment: Has done well on AW since fitted with tongue-tie/cheekpieces; up 4lb for a defeat, though

runners.name: Davorge Nation
runners.spotlight: First (5f, good) and a close third (6f, AW) in nurseries before a quiet run on good to soft at Musselburgh; returns on a competitive mark and after a wind operation; could feature.
runners.comment: Nursery winner; returns on a competitive mark and after a wind operation; could feature

races.race_name: Hugh James Classified Stakes

runners.name: Shes Got The Blues
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden but she's reached the frame in three of her last four runs including when fourth of 14 in a Windsor handicap (1m, good to firm) last time; should get a decent pace on this drop back in trip and she should go well again.
runners.comment: 0-14 but she's reached the frame in three of her last four runs; respected back in trip

runners.name: Shark Two One
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been on turf and latest was in this race last year; his form nosedived at Doncaster last month but he has a striking record at this track and is versatile ground-wise; big player if he can bounce back.
runners.comment: Won this last year and he's a big player if he can bounce back after a blip at Doncaster

runners.name: Kode Grey
runners.spotlight: Improved form when a front-running third in Ripon handicap (6f, good) last month but she failed to back that up at Carlisle ten days ago; still lightly raced but she has bit to prove again back up in trip.
runners.comment: Five-race maiden who was disappointing at Carlisle ten days ago; bit to prove again

runners.name: Squashy Berry
runners.spotlight: Has struggled in his three runs this year and was beaten 20l in a Lingfield maiden (7f, good) last time; still early days but he needs blinkers to make a big difference on this drop into a classified event.
runners.comment: Still early days but he needs to show a lot more on this drop into a classified event

runners.name: Bungle Bay
runners.spotlight: All eight wins have been at Wolverhampton and he went close in a classified event there (7f) last Tuesday; record of 0-12 on turf but he has placed form in this sphere and he seems fairly versatile ground-wise; key player.
runners.comment: Went close at Wolverhampton latest and is a big player if he can repeat that form

runners.name: Tilsworth Max
runners.spotlight: Plenty of good efforts on AW during the winter, including a win at Lingfield (7f) in January; that form puts him in the picture but he hasn't threatened back on turf in last two runs and overall record is now 1-29.
runners.comment: Overall record of 1-29 and he hasn't threatened back on turf in last two runs

runners.name: Demetris Mouflon
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly but she's been well held in most of her runs including when a 12l fifth in Lingfield handicap (7f, good; blinkers added) last month; that was after eighth months off but she needs improvement on this drop into a classified event.
runners.comment: Lightly raced filly but she has a best RPR of 44 and others are preferred

runners.name: Tickhill Flyer
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to three winners but he's struggled at big odds in three runs (5f-7f) this spring; now drops into a classified event but he needs a transformation with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Unexposed gelding but he needs improvement switched to a classified event; headgear added

runners.name: Eulalia
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a good third in a Kempton handicap (7f, AW) for Charlie Fellowes; looks interesting on that form but she's struggled in both runs for current yard and is now 0-7; blinkers added.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden and she's struggled in both runs for current yard; blinkers added

runners.name: Due Destiny
runners.spotlight: Poor form in four 2yo runs for Archie Watson and was a well-held fifth in Lingfield handicap (6f, AW; hood added) on stable debut in April; should benefit from that run but she needs a major step forward at this new trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo but she needs a major step forward at this new trip

runners.name: Coverbridge
runners.spotlight: Sole win was in a Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) two years ago and he's been well held in his three runs season; this big drop back in trip could help but others are more convincing; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: Sole win was two years ago and he's been well held in three runs this season

runners.name: Bridgefoot Rambler
runners.spotlight: Struggled at big prices in her three runs including in two starts over 1m (good/AW) this spring; now drops into a classified event but she needs a transformation with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly but she needs a transformation dropped into a classified race; headgear on

runners.name: Miss Helena
runners.spotlight: Struggled in two bumpers last autumn and same story at massive prices in four Flat runs this year; equipment now returns but she has stacks to find on the figures and can only be watched.
runners.comment: Struggled in two bumpers and same story at massive prices in four Flat runs this year

races.race_name: Mesothelioma UK Handicap (GBBplus Race)

runners.name: Zatsgood
runners.spotlight: Ex-French gelding; all four runs have been on AW and he found more progress when landing the odds on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m) last month; up 4lb but he still looks on a workable mark and should be unfazed by this step up to 1m2f; strongly respected on turf debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who won on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) last month; respected up 4lb

runners.name: Ted Le Saux
runners.spotlight: Won a Newcastle novice (1m, AW) in January but was down the field on handicap/turf debut at Haydock (10.4f, good to firm) in April; remains unexposed after only four starts but needs to resume his progress.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but he needs to kick on again to defy this mark

runners.name: Seagolazo
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner who is 0-12 in handicaps but he's on a reduced mark and ran respectably in a Class 3 at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) two weeks ago; has possibilities if he can find something extra and he won't mind what the weather does.
runners.comment: 0-12 in handicaps but he's on a reduced mark and ran respectably in a Class 3 last time
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Percy's Daydream
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-9 on turf but she finished a remote last of four in her bid for a double at Catterick (1m4f, good) last month; has bit to prove again after that and it would be a worry if rain arrives (almost all of her runs have been on good/good to firm.)
runners.comment: Disappointing at Catterick last time and it would be a worry if rain arrives

runners.name: Noble Horizon
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D novice (good to firm) for William Haggas last July and was third of 19 in a York handicap (10.3f, good) in his final run last season; well held on stable debut at Lingfield last month but he shaped as though he needed the run and that form has been well advertised by the first two since; dangerous if he can get back near best.
runners.comment: 4yo who is still unexposed in handicaps and has claims if he can get back near best

runners.name: Double Red
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and good to soft; reappeared with a comfortable AW win in April but he's not easy to predict and was tailed off back on turf at Lingfield (1m2f, good to firm) last month; needs to bounce back again after that but he still looks feasibly treated on his penultimate form.
runners.comment: Won on reappearance in April but he flopped back on turf last time; risks attached

runners.name: Crystal Mariner
runners.spotlight: Triple AW winner who completed a Kempton double (1m3f, AW) in October but he's been well held in two starts since; may have needed his reappearance run at Chepstow last month but that took his turf record to 1-13 and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Triple AW winner but he's 1-13 on turf and was well held at Chepstow last month

runners.name: The Green Mile
runners.spotlight: Has mixed record in his six starts but he added to his debut win for the Crisfords when landing a Chepstow handicap (1m2f, good to soft; hood applied) last month; up 4lb but he beat a clear second last time and he still has potential; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Made it 2-6 when scoring off 4lb lower a Chepstow last month; shortlisted

runners.name: Nowshesdancing
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in Dundalk maiden (10.6f, AW) for Mark Fahey in January; reappeared for new yard with a third at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) in April and that was a creditable effort behind a 4-9 shot; should have more to offer now switched to handicapping and she's in the mix.
runners.comment: Win and third in her two qualifying runs and she needs a close look on handicap debut

runners.name: Woodstock City
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m3f Class 5 handicap at Southwell in October and slightly better form when collared late over 1m2f at Lingfield in January; broke out of stalls and withdrawn at Kempton 11 days later (10-1 at the time); now returns to turf after a break but he's a dual winner in this sphere and is versatile ground-wise; possibilities.
runners.comment: Four-time winner who is versatile ground-wise and he's in the mix on his return

runners.name: Tipsy Tiger
runners.spotlight: His last win was at Sandown (1m2f, soft) in September 2024 but he went close at Nottingham on his penultimate run last season; ideally suited by slow ground and could be dangerous if conditions are suitable on his return.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark and he needs watching in market back from 231 days off

races.race_name: Smooth FM Ladies' Day Saturday 4th July Book Now Classified Stakes

runners.name: A Lott Of Kane
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the eighth attempt when justifying favouritism in a Wolverhampton handicap (5f, AW) in March; held in fourth in a classified event there last Monday but that was a creditable effort from a wide draw; now returns to turf but he's unexposed in this sphere and is a big player if he can run near his best.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton on penultimate run and he's unexposed on turf; respected

runners.name: Dreambird Dolly
runners.spotlight: 0-18 but she bounced back with a near-miss in new headgear at Brighton (5.3f handicap) ten days ago; has leading claims if she can repeat that form and she seems fairly versatile ground-wise.
runners.comment: 0-18 but she had a near-miss at Brighton last time; big player if she can back that up
runners.owner: T Beeches, C Stone, J Gallagher

runners.name: Antiphon
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and soft; his last win was in April 2024 but he was much better than this level in his time and he ran several good races at Brighton (5.3f) last season; returns after 235 days off but he needs a close look for in-form yard.
runners.comment: His last win was in 2024 but he's on a dangerous mark and needs a close look on his return

runners.name: Bridget's Baby
runners.spotlight: Struggled at massive prices in maiden/novice events and latest was on her reappearance here (7f, good to firm) last month; still early days and now drops into a classified contest but she needs a transformation back in trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly but she needs a transformation on this drop into a classified event

runners.name: Ziva's Star
runners.spotlight: 20-race maiden who has finished down the field in her three runs (5f-7f) this season; cheekpieces are now added but this looks a tough assignment on the figures and others are preferred.
runners.comment: 20-race maiden who has finished down the field in her three runs this term; headgear added

runners.name: Dunnington Lad
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 5f at Ripon in 2024 but last year was a struggle and he's been beaten 11l and 13l in his two runs this season; has lots to prove and he needs a major revival with tongue-tie reapplied.
runners.comment: Last year was a struggle and he's been beaten 11l and 13l in two runs this season

runners.name: Tilsworth Ony Ta
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner but his last success was in July 2024 and he has form figures of 879090 this year; opposable.
runners.comment: Five-time winner but his last success was in 2024 and he has record of 879090 this year

runners.name: Rohini
runners.spotlight: Poor form in three 2yo runs over this trip for Robert Cowell; now drops into a classified event but she returns after nine months off and needs major improvement for new yard.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo but she needs major improvement on return for new yard

runners.name: Dottie Diamond
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly who has finished ahead of only five rivals in her five starts and latest was in a Chelmsford handicap (5f, AW) in November 2024; can only be watched back from 576 days off.
runners.comment: Out the back in all five starts and can only be watched after 576 days off

races.race_name: Garvey's Bar, Ballintubber Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Cinating
runners.spotlight: Decent level of form in bumpers (including on testing ground); in the process of running a big race on hurdles debut at Ballinrobe when falling two out; should go well here if that hasn't dented her confidence; hood goes on.
runners.comment: Running a big race when falling two out on hurdles debut, should go well, hooded

runners.name: Ritz Plan
runners.spotlight: Promise in all three starts in academy hurdles on testing ground for Joesph O'Brien; off since 2.25l second at Naas in December (2m1f, sft-hvy); could go well on first run for new yard.
runners.comment: Promise in academy hurdles for former yard, off since Dec', soft ground fine, go well

runners.name: Take The Free
runners.spotlight: Sent off at big prices both starts; well beaten on debut at Cork (16.5f, yielding) but much better when a 7l sixth of 14 in another maiden hurdle (17.5f, good) back there on latest; place claims if backing that up but softer ground a query (dam won on soft-heavy).
runners.comment: Much improved on second start at Cork, place claims if backs that up, soft an unknown

runners.name: Tex Amare
runners.spotlight: Showed ability on sole start on the Flat in Germany at Munich (1m2f, soft) last October; that form isn't easy to assess and she is hooded for her hurdling debut; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Good run sole Flat start in Germany, hard to assess, hood for hurdles debut, check market

runners.name: Mike Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth in Leopardstown bumper on debut in March (2m, sft-hvy); failed to back it up when tailed off at Kilbeggan (2m1f, gd-yld); half-brother to a hurdles winner and return to softer ground a likely plus.
runners.comment: Promise in bumper on debut on testing ground, tailed off on better ground latest

runners.name: Neat As A Pin
runners.spotlight: Homebred Getaway filly; sister to point winner Sound As A Bell; dam point winner, closely related to high class jumpers Follow The Plan and Ballychorus; nicely bred and needs checking in the market on debut.
runners.comment: Getaway filly, dam closely related to classy jumpers, worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Teffian Warrior
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden rated 64; not an obvious candidate for hurdling on breeding; tongue-tie added to cheekpieces; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden, not bred to make a hurdler, new headgear combo, likely best watched

runners.name: Jacks Bridge
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and failed to beat a rival in Punchestown maiden hurdle last month; tongue-tie goes on; impossible to fancy.
runners.comment: Tailed off at 66-1 on debut at Punchestown, tongue-tie on, impossible to fancy

runners.name: Mytommy
runners.spotlight: Castle Du Berlais gelding; half-brother to winners Enrichissant (2m4f hurdle/chase), Bambouzle (1m4f-1m7f AQPS Flat) and Don't Hesitate (12.5f/1m5f AQPS Flat and 2m hurdle); dam placed 1m4f-1m7f AQPS Flat; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Castle Du Berlais gelding, bred to have a future over hurdles, check market

runners.name: Dont Break My Word
runners.spotlight: Well held in maiden hurdles at Sligo and Ballinrobe; fourth last time but 25l behind the winner; will probably be seen to better effect when handicapping.
runners.comment: Well held in both maiden hurdles, beaten 25l last time, probably one for handicaps

runners.name: Who's Da Goose
runners.spotlight: 100-1 and weakened into a 37l seventh of 14 on debut at Killarney (2m1f gd-yld); seems safe to rule out after that.
runners.comment: 100-1 and weakened into a 37l seventh of 14 on debut at Killarney, safe to rule out

runners.name: God The Highness
runners.spotlight: Modest on the Flat; ordinary efforts both starts over hurdles; fifth at Limerick last time was a remote one; others preferred; declared to run on Flat at Navan 4.37 Sunday.
runners.comment: Modest on the Flat, well beaten both starts over hurdles, others preferred

runners.name: Tribal
runners.spotlight: Unlikely winner based on heavy maiden hurdle defeats at Killarney and Ballinrobe.
runners.comment: Unlikely winner based on heavy maiden hurdle defeats at Killarney and Ballinrobe

runners.name: Betterluckthistime
runners.spotlight: 100-1 and ran like it on debut in a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle in April; unlikely.
runners.comment: 100-1 and tailed off in Kilbeggan maiden hurdle on debut, unlikely winner

races.race_name: McNulty Furniture Rated Novice Hurdle

runners.name: Berto Ramirez
runners.spotlight: Limited Flat maiden but doing better over hurdles and after winning his maiden at Down Royal he ran well at the weights in a rated hurdle at Wexford; has form claims for sure but this softer ground might not be preferable, especially over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Has form claims for sure but this softer ground might not be preferable
runners.owner: D O'Mahony & D McHugh & D J Kelly & G Butler

runners.name: Chanceawetmorning
runners.spotlight: Has soon improved for Gordon Elliott in winning two 2m handicaps and the defeat upped to 2m4f at Listowel was no backward step; all those runs were on good to yielding, but handles soft; hard to knock.
runners.comment: Form figures of 113 in handicaps for current yard; the defeat was no backward step

runners.name: Polepatrick
runners.spotlight: His second run back from an absence when breezing home at odds-on in a 2m soft-ground maiden at Tramore; he no doubt has plenty more to offer; this longer trip should be up his street.
runners.comment: Breezed home at odds-on in a 2m soft-ground maiden at Tramore

races.race_name: Dermot Hughes Car Sales Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Eye Of A Tiger
runners.spotlight: Has lost her way since winning a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle last August; beaten 24l in mares' handicap hurdle at Ballinrobe latest (2m, good); others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Lost way since maiden hurdle win, beaten 24l at Ballinrobe latest, others preferred

runners.name: Stede Bonnet
runners.spotlight: Dual-bumper winner is 2-6 over hurdles; won a Tramore maiden hurdle on New Year's Day (2m1f, soft) and Ayr novice (2m, soft) the following month; failed to fire upped to 20.5f (soft) in the Martin Pipe at Cheltenham; off since and capable of better; not discounted.
runners.comment: 2-6 over hurdles, disappointed at Cheltenham but too soon to write off, can't discount

runners.name: Ollie La Ba Ba
runners.spotlight: Won second race over hurdles when landing a handicap at Listowel (2m2f, gd-yld) in September; below best twice since, in a handicap chase at Clonmel and a handicap hurdle on heavy at Limerick in December; off since and record fresh suggests he might need this run.
runners.comment: Won off 5lb lower at Listowel in Sept', heavy ground excuse latest, may need this

runners.name: Rakki
runners.spotlight: Won his maiden hurdle here in September (15.5f, gd-yld); creditable run when second in a handicap hurdle at Punchestown in November; disappointed both starts since, including when beaten 30l at Punchestown on return from break in April; sharper for that and tongue-tie goes on now.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle win came here, beaten 30l on return from break latest, tongue-tie on

runners.name: Avalo
runners.spotlight: Gained second win over hurdles on return from a 168-day break in gutsy fashion at the Punchestown festival (2m, yielding) off 7lb lower; won his maiden over 2m4f so this longer trip fine but the forecast rain is a big concern (flopped both previous starts on soft).
runners.comment: Gutsy 2m win at Punchestown after break latest, up 7lb, stays 2m4f, rain a big concern
runners.owner: Seamus Perry & Brian P Larkin & Ms Bernadette Leigh

runners.name: Sequoiaspirit
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner (including here) is 1-28 over hurdles; last two runs in this sphere poor but shaped well on the level at Killarney four weeks ago; just 1lb above sole hurdles win so place chance but hard to fancy for win purposes given his modest strike-rate.
runners.comment: 1-28 over hurdles, nice run on Flat latest, just 1lb above last win, place shout

runners.name: Simple Things
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly is 0-6 over hurdles but has races in her; runner up in mares' handicaps at Bellewstown (2m1f, yielding) and Wexford (2m4f, gd-yld) the last twice; nudged up another 3lb and not sure she wants testing ground; chance if the forecast rain misses.
runners.comment: 4yo filly, 0-6 but good runs in defeat the last twice, needs the forecast rain to miss

runners.name: Billy Lee Swagger
runners.spotlight: Gained third win over timber when bolting up in 22.5f handicap at Ballinrobe (good) two weeks ago; rider's claim negates most of 9lb rise for that 8l win; ground versatile and should go well again.
runners.comment: Bolted up at Ballinrobe latest, rider's claim negates most of 9lb hike, go well again

runners.name: Sir Allen
runners.spotlight: 2-13 over timber; returned from mammoth layoff with decisive Flat win at Navan in March (1m6f, heavy); given time to get over those exertions and returns to hurdling off what may be a workable mark; could go well.
runners.comment: Back from long layoff with Navan Flat win in March, hurdles mark could be workable

runners.name: Lisnagar Fortune
runners.spotlight: Punchestown bumper and maiden hurdle winner for Willie Mullins; 150-1 and tailed off on debut for this stable in Grade 3 Red Mills Hurdle at Gowran in February; off since and hard to know what to expect after that; watch.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut for this yard in Red Mills at Gowran, hard to assess now, watch
runners.owner: Keep Er Lit Syndicate/Donal McArdle/Pink Pony Club Syndicate

runners.name: Dreal Deal
runners.spotlight: Veteran has rediscovered form in recent starts; fine third in veterans' handicap chase at Haydock (2m4f, good) in April; another solid effort in veteran's handicap chase at Ballinrobe latest (19.5f, good); rated 3lb lower over hurdles but hasn't won in this sphere since 2021.
runners.comment: In good form in veterans' chases recently, no win over hurdles since 2021
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Philip Smith & Nigel O'Hare & P J Rothwell

races.race_name: McGowan Accountancy Services Novice Chase

runners.name: Workahead
runners.spotlight: Rated 144 over hurdles; made perfect start to chasing career when lowering the colours of Supersundae in a novice at Wexford (2m, gd-yld); 5lb penalty for that but handles softer ground and the one to beat.
runners.comment: 144 rated hurdler, impressive win on chase debut, 5lb penalty but the one to beat

runners.name: Reiki Revolution
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; 1-8 over hurdles and rated 112; was a long way behind when unseating two out on chase debut in the Wexford novice won by Workahead; likely up against it again here.
runners.comment: Was a long way back when unseating in novice chase won by Workahead, up against it

runners.name: Bannow Blaze
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler rated 112 in that sphere; unseated at the first on chase debut at Tramore; best watched for now.
runners.comment: 112 rated hurdler, unseated at the first on chase debut at Tramore, best watched

runners.name: Scalpnagoon
runners.spotlight: 122 rated hurdler has shown ability in all three chase starts but jumping errors have held him back; beaten 11l when runner up behind Net Boy at Ballinrobe latest (2m1f, good); place claims again.
runners.comment: 122 rated hurdler, jumping has been an issue in three chase starts, place claims

runners.name: Talk In The Park
runners.spotlight: The most experienced chaser in the field but is 0-9; beaten 11.5l off 125 in Navan handicap chase (2m4f, heavy) when last seen in December; goes well fresh but best form on good ground so wouldn't want to see much rain.
runners.comment: 0-9 over fences and rated 125, off since Dec', goes well fresh, best on good ground

runners.name: Wild Bill Hickok
runners.spotlight: Talented hurdler (rated 135); foot perfect start to chase career when landing a Wexford beginners' by 11l last month; proved no match for Workahead in novice chase back there on latest; supporting role likely again.
runners.comment: Won on chase debut, no match for Workahead latest, supporting role likely again

runners.name: Haveigotnewsforyou
runners.spotlight: Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner rated 120 in that sphere; jumping issues both chase starts; beaten 63l in beginners at Gowran in January and unseated at Leopardstown latest in March; risky proposition for now.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner, jumping issues both chase starts, risky proposition for now

runners.name: Positive Energy
runners.spotlight: Flat and maiden hurdle winner for Michael O'Callaghan; hard to fancy on the back of two runs for this stable; beaten 56l when 150-1 in Ballinrobe beginners' chase latest.
runners.comment: Beaten 56l when 150-1 in Ballinrobe beginners' chase latest, hard to fancy

races.race_name: Bet With Tote.ie On Racing & Sports Connacht National Handicap Chase

runners.name: Rocky's Howya
runners.spotlight: 1-7 over fences; has won four of last five starts, including his last run in a handicap chase at Limerick in March (2m7f, heavy); just 3lb higher here and advertised well-being with a claiming hurdle win at Clonmel 25 days ago (19.5f, gd-yld); not out of this.
runners.comment: Won off 3lb lower at Limerick in March, claiming hurdle win latest, not out of this

runners.name: Born Bright
runners.spotlight: Won three races last year, including handicap chases at Tipperary (2m7f, yielding) and Market Rasen (3m, good); is 6lb higher than for that latter win; shaped nicely on return from a break over hurdles at Wexford 23 days ago but soft ground would be a big concern for her.
runners.comment: Two chase wins last year, pleasing run over hurdles after break latest, needs rain to miss

runners.name: Bartlemy Boy
runners.spotlight: Prolific point-to-pointer (5-11); won a Listowel hunter chase on latest, his second win under rules; trip and ground should be fine on his handicap debut but tricky to assess whether he's well treated off this mark; probably up against it.
runners.comment: 5-11 in points, hunter chase win latest, h'cap debut, hard to assess, may be up against it

runners.name: Will Do
runners.spotlight: Bumper and hurdles winner is 0-16 over fences; has run some smart races in defeat in valuable handicaps but has pulled up in three of his last four starts so comes with plenty of risks attached now; on a dangerous mark if rediscovers best.
runners.comment: 0-16 over fences, smart form in defeat but pulled up three of last four starts, risky

runners.name: Rocky's Diamond
runners.spotlight: Classy hurdler made a bright start to his chasing career with a beginners' win at Galway (2m2f, gd-yld) followed by listed chase second at Wexford (2m7f, gd-yld); came up short in a grade 2 novice chase at Punchestown in November; poor back over hurdles the last twice and now makes handicap chase debut after 137 days off; market can guide.
runners.comment: Classy hurdler, has shown ability over fences, makes h'cap chase debut, check market

runners.name: Boston Rover
runners.spotlight: Failed to win over timber but better over fences, winning three from five starts including a 2m5f (good) beginners' here; won on handicap chase debut at Gowran last October off 125 (2m4f, yielding) before pulled up off revised mark of 134 in the Munster National (3m, yld-sft) in October; off since and could do with the track drying out.
runners.comment: Won beginners' here (2m5f), pulled up on soft in Munster National, needs track to dry out

runners.name: Where's My Jet
runners.spotlight: Maiden and novice hurdle winner is 0-8 over fences and yet to finish in the first three; didn't run too badly in grade 3 handicap at the DRF in February but beaten 45l and pulled up both starts since; stamina to prove for this trip too; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-8 over fences, beaten 45l and pulled up last two starts, stamina query, others preferred

runners.name: Goody Four Shoes
runners.spotlight: Shed the maiden tag on fourth chase start in a Down Royal handicap last month (3m2f, good); 5lb rise for that seems fair but this is a much tougher race and has to prove effectiveness on softer ground.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Down Royal latest, up 5lb, this harder and softer ground is a query

runners.name: Smallcraftwarning
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; won at Killarney (2m7f, yld-sft) last summer off 91 and posted some fine efforts in defeat subsequently; not a bad effort at Limerick on latest but 12lb wrong here and this looks too demanding.
runners.comment: Some good runs in defeat since Killarney win, 12lb wrong, this too demanding; reserve

runners.name: Pride Of Place
runners.spotlight: Had been out of sorts on soft ground earlier this year but roared back to top form when bolting up in the Killarney National a month ago (3m2f, gd-yld); 9lb rise for that seems fair but may not get his favoured underfoot conditions for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Back to form with easy Killarney National win latest, best form on good ground

runners.name: Ambitious Fellow
runners.spotlight: 4-16 over hurdles but yet to win over the bigger obstacles (0-11); out of sorts this year but he was a 1.25l runner up in this race last year off this mark at 25-1 and he was running a better race when unseating at Listowel last time.
runners.comment: Runner up in this last year, same mark now, signs of revival when unseating latest

runners.name: Harbour Highway
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; in good form this spring bar a blip when pulled up on soft ground at Fairyhouse; won at Leopardstown in March (21.5f, yld) and then runner up at Kilbeggan (2m4f, gd-yld) and Limerick (24.5f, good); 2lb wrong here but rider's claim negates that; cheekpieces added; forecast rain is a concern.
runners.comment: In good form, runner up last twice on good ground, forecast rain a concern; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Tonaghneave Well
runners.spotlight: Two-time winning hurdler rated 10lb higher in that sphere but that is for a reason; heavy defeats in all five chase starts and best form on good ground so the forecast rain is a worry; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Rated 10lb higher over hurdles, heavy defeats all five chases, others appeal more

runners.name: Kings Hill
runners.spotlight: Best form on soft and heavy ground; won a novice handicap chase at Limerick in March (2m2f, heavy) off 117; stayed on for a 10l third of 17 over 2m5f (yld-sft) at Fairyhouse in April off revised mark of 124; worth another go at this sort of trip and the more rain that falls the better his chance will be.
runners.comment: Best on soft, stayed on over 2m5f latest, worth another go at this trip, rain a plus

runners.name: Killaney King
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner yet to score under Rules but placed on multiple occasions, including when a good second on final start for Henry De Bromhead at Fairyhouse in April (24.5f, yld-sft); just 1lb higher now and can't be discounted on first start for new stable.
runners.comment: Placed all three chase starts, good second at Fairyhouse latest, new yard, can't discount

runners.name: Hermino AA
runners.spotlight: Useful three-time winning hurdler in Britain; yet to show much since joining this yard and extended chase record to 0-8 when beaten 31l in the Killarney National last time; 2lb drop in the weights and new blinkers not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Extended chase record to 0-8 at Killarney, 2lb drop and blinkers not enough to tempt

runners.name: A Penny A Hundred
runners.spotlight: Displayed credentials for a race of this nature when a 12l third of 15 in the Mayo National at Ballinrobe last year off 132; ordinary form on last three starts last autumn and off the track since but now 3lb lower than she was at Ballinrobe and she needs checking in the market on return for top yard.
runners.comment: Third in last year's Mayo National off 3lb higher, off since Nov', check the market

runners.name: Dorking Cock
runners.spotlight: First reserve; 3-20 over fences but no win of any description under Rules since November 2023; soundly beaten in Downpatrick hunter chase on latest; 4lb wrong here and others preferred.
runners.comment: Long losing run, well beaten in hunter chase latest, 4lb wrong, others preferred; reserve

runners.name: Prince Palace
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; 4lb wrong; won listed novice handicap at Navan last year but has lost way; pulled up in three of six starts since seasonal return in October; beaten 26l on first run for this stable at Leopardstown in March; risky proposition.
runners.comment: Pulled up in three of last six starts, beaten 26l on stable debut latest, risky; reserve

races.race_name: Sweeney Oil Handicap Chase

runners.name: Stoneyford Lady
runners.spotlight: 6lb higher than for her win in a 2m2f handicap chase at Thurles but was running well when clouting two out next time; latest run needs forgiving but she has a big effort in her.
runners.comment: Latest run needs forgiving but she has a big effort in her; won three starts back

runners.name: Spanish Maid
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over fences at the ninth attempt in a 1m7f handicap at Tramore (in cheekpieces) and then narrowly followed up back over hurdles (no headgear); higher mark to contend with but she's a thriving mare with a feather weight.
runners.comment: Higher mark to contend with but she's a thriving mare with a feather weight

runners.name: Chosen Shant
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back handicap hurdles at Wexford last summer; chase form (0-6) lacks substance but she's potentially well in given hurdling exploits.
runners.comment: Chase form lacks substance but she's potentially well in given hurdling exploits

runners.name: Donnrua Dream
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner but yet to deliver over fences (0-9) despite being strong in the betting on occasions; jumped moderately 18 days ago.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdle winner but yet to deliver over fences; jumped moderately last time

runners.name: L'Amiral Fromentin
runners.spotlight: Only had the eight races, seven of those over fences; knocking on the door but has questions to answer on this slower ground.
runners.comment: Knocking on the door over fences but has questions to answer on this slower ground

runners.name: Soldante
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since winning his maiden hurdle in 2023; has returned from a long absence with three competitive runs over fences, the last two both handicaps; trip/ground fine; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Back from an absence with three competitive runs over fences, the last two both handicaps
runners.owner: P McCormack & David P McCormack & Sarah Glynn

runners.name: Struck Oil
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo was who runner-up in his final handicap over hurdles (2m1f; 2lb lower); sent off favourite for chase debut off this mark at Downpatrick and ran okay to be beaten 13l into third having jumped deliberately; if sharper for that experience he can go well.
runners.comment: Jumped deliberately when third on chase debut; could be sharper this time

runners.name: Presenting Lad
runners.spotlight: Six wins include two over fences and was runner-up at Tramore ten days ago on good ground; handles these conditions.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Tramore ten days ago on good ground; handles these conditions

runners.name: Moylussa
runners.spotlight: A winner over hurdles and now goes chasing on the back of a 2l third in a 2m handicap at Ballinrobe; handles soft ground and he has a pointing background.
runners.comment: Goes chasing on the back of a 2l third in a 2m handicap at Ballinrobe

runners.name: Piccolo Player
runners.spotlight: Won his maiden hurdle on heavy ground and has since been competitive off this sort of mark; now goes over fences and there are chasers in the family.
runners.comment: Respected on the pick of his hurdle form and there are chasers in the family

runners.name: Must Go Now
runners.spotlight: Won a Fairyhouse handicap hurdle (2m4f, yielding to soft) in early 2025 but only one effort of note in the past 12 months, when third in first-time cheekpieces at Cork in March; tailed off in both his beginners' chases.
runners.comment: Mostly disappointing over the past 12 months; tailed off in his two beginners' chases

runners.name: Katherine
runners.spotlight: Ran surprisingly well behind the classy Murcia in a mares' hurdle last week, but on balance of form it's hard to take that effort literally; this is a belated return to fences (0-5).
runners.comment: Probably flattered in a hurdle race nine days ago; this is a belated return to fences

runners.name: Keep On Dreaming
runners.spotlight: 2-6 in points and 0-4 in hunter chases, with all that form at trips between 2m6f and 3m1f; hard to know how he'll fare over this far in his first handicap and there have to be concerns.
runners.comment: 2-6 in points and 0-4 in hunter chases, with all that form at trips between 2m6f and 3m1f

runners.name: Party President
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner but signed off for Tony Mullins on a low note and was pulled up on recent stable/chase debut.
runners.comment: Signed off for Tony Mullins on a low note and was pulled up on recent stable/chase debut

races.race_name: Roscommon (Q.R.) Handicap Chase

runners.name: Ballagh Star
runners.spotlight: Tailed off after a break last month but that was over hurdles and chasing is more his game; chance on his competitive form last autumn but wants further ideally.
runners.comment: Chance on his competitive form last autumn but wants further ideally

runners.name: Finnicky Filly
runners.spotlight: Hard to win with but not in bad form by any means, over fences and hurdles; being unraced over this far is a likely negative.
runners.comment: Hard to win with but not in bad form by any means, over fences and hurdles

runners.name: Kilashee
runners.spotlight: Better hurdler than chaser; she is at least back off her correct mark this time but wouldn't be the percentage call; now an 11yo.
runners.comment: Better hurdler than chaser; she is at least back off her correct mark this time

runners.name: Ta Se Rua
runners.spotlight: Point and hunter chase winner; latest third at Tramore (hunter chase) was quite remote; opposable back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Point and hunter chase winner; latest third at Tramore (hunter chase) was quite remote

runners.name: Stormy Master
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner; veteran who was returning from a lengthy break when only 2l away at Limerick (2m6f, good) but this ground could be softer than he cares for.
runners.comment: Pleasing return from a break at Limerick but this softer surface won't be ideal

runners.name: Rideau Canal
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m5f handicap chase in the mud at Tramore in February but nothing since over fences and hurdles.
runners.comment: Won at Tramore in February but nothing since over fences and hurdles

runners.name: Jouster
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner for Gavin Cromwell before losing his way; some signs of a revival for his new yard, latterly behind a sharp improver at Tramore; a tongue-tie returns with new blinkers added.
runners.comment: Some signs of a revival for his new yard, latterly behind a sharp improver at Tramore

runners.name: The Rebel County
runners.spotlight: Fairly slim pickings since winning a hunter chase more than a year ago; beaten 18l last week; tried in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Fairly slim pickings since winning a hunter chase more than a year ago

runners.name: Revolut Ned
runners.spotlight: After winning a point he ran a solid third in a 2m6f handicap at Tramore nine days ago, beaten 7l by a well-backed winner; only a 7yo and considered.
runners.comment: After winning a point he ran a solid third in a 2m6f handicap at Tramore nine days ago

runners.name: Bohemian Bay
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in two handicaps over hurdles; his places in points suggest he may do better over fences and he's only a 6yo.
runners.comment: His places in points suggest he may do better over fences and he's only a 6yo

runners.name: Silent Flight
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles in early 2024; offered little in four runs over fences before another heavy defeat back hurdling.
runners.comment: Offered little in four runs over fences before another heavy defeat back hurdling

runners.name: Kilkee Bay
runners.spotlight: Couldn't win over hurdles for Gordon Elliott; it's been an underwhelming start over fences and others have more pressing claims; the return of blinkers doesn't look significant.
runners.comment: Couldn't win over hurdles for Gordon Elliott; it's been an underwhelming start over fences
runners.owner: Aiden Larkin & John Larkin & Paudie Dowling & Kenneth Fox

runners.name: Ballingurteen
runners.spotlight: Second in a point so could make a chaser and there's been definite improvement in her last two runs over hurdles, both handicaps at about 2m3f; could be interesting.
runners.comment: Improvement over hurdles in recent weeks and she could well make a chaser

runners.name: Cavalry Master
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdle/chase winner but a veteran now and nothing like the force of old; opposable.
runners.comment: Bumper/hurdle/chase winner but a veteran now and nothing like the force of old; opposable
runners.owner: Peter Maher & M J Halligan & Jeffrey Brennan & Alan E Smith

runners.name: Made U Look
runners.spotlight: His form in hunter chases isn't overly compelling and probably best watched on this return to rules action, despite winning his latest point.
runners.comment: Probably best watched on this return to rules action, despite winning his latest point

runners.name: Robin Elite
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has struggled in handicap chases and recent pointing exploits do not bode well.
runners.comment: Reserve; has struggled in handicap chases and recent pointing exploits do not bode well

runners.name: Annie Questions
runners.spotlight: Reserve; belatedly off the mark in points last month; once placed over hurdles but two years ago and was tailed off in a handicap on previous run over regulation fences; hood added.
runners.comment: Reserve; belatedly off the mark in points last month; opposable back under rules

runners.name: Eddies Pride
runners.spotlight: Reserve; regressive mare; 80-1 when tailed off in a Limerick handicap 18 days ago.
runners.comment: Reserve; regressive mare; 80-1 when tailed off in a Limerick handicap 18 days ago

runners.name: Global Assembly
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over regulation fences and this year's pointing exploits are nothing to shout about, last time finishing well behind Revolut Ned.
runners.comment: 0-8 over regulation fences and this year's pointing exploits are nothing to shout about

races.race_name: Roscommon Livestock Mart (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Tell The Boys
runners.spotlight: Confirmed previous promise when winning in a canter at Listowel (good to yielding; second run in a hood) and in receipt of weight she might well give Linford something to think about; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Confirmed previous promise when winning in a canter at Listowel nine days ago

runners.name: Linford
runners.spotlight: 9-4 favourite when comfortably pulling 10l clear at Killarney (good to yielding); that form has yet to be tested but this 4yo looked good and probably has a bright future.
runners.comment: 9-4 favourite when comfortably pulling 10l clear at Killarney; looks a good prospect

runners.name: Fleu Du Glaye
runners.spotlight: Beaten 18l by Linford at Killarney and there's no clear reason why he should be reversing those placings.
runners.comment: Beaten 18l by Linford at Killarney and shouldn't be reversing those placings

runners.name: Hilly Filly
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros 3yo; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Fascile Mode (useful bumper and 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase) and C'Est Rien (2m7f chase); dam 2m4f hurdle winner; bred to have a future.
