runners.comment: 16,000euros 3yo; related to winners so bred to have a future; betting can guide

runners.name: Caislean Gear
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam unraced half-sister to smart French/US middle-distance performer Rhythm Mad and 1m3f Flat and 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner No Greater Love; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Dam unraced half-sister to smart French/US middle-distance performer Rhythm Mad

runners.name: Familiar Game
runners.spotlight: Cost only 2,600gns as 3yo; second foal; half-brother to Greek/Polish 5f/6f winner Mollys Pride; dam 6f/7f maiden (RPR 68); wouldn't be the obvious answer.
runners.comment: Cost only 2,600gns as 3yo; half-brother to Greek/Polish 5f/6f winner Mollys Pride

runners.name: Skylab
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam runner-up first of two starts in bumpers, out of 1m winning half-sister to Listed-placed dams of Group 1 winners Qemah (French 1m), Ask Victor More (Japanese 1m7f) and Pretty Gorgeous (1m 2yo); the market should be enlightening with the yard also represented by Linford.
runners.comment: Newcomer; market enlightening with the yard also represented by Linford

runners.name: Coolnagrattan
runners.spotlight: Some promise when fourth at Punchestown (good to yielding; in this headgear combination) but the RPR was modest.
runners.comment: Some promise when fourth at Punchestown but the RPR was modest

runners.name: Knocknasuff
runners.spotlight: 100-1 and completely tailed off in Linford's race at Killarney.
runners.comment: 100-1 and completely tailed off in Linford's race at Killarney

races.race_name: Royal Jersey Laundry Clean Sweep Handicap

runners.name: Charlie Boyo
runners.spotlight: Useful debut run at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) last May; turned over at a short price on only subsequent 2yo start but he made some late progress at Salisbury recently when returning from an  absence; on a fair mark on handicap debut if debut effort is taken at face value; now tongue tied.
runners.comment: Useful 2yo debut; disappointed next time; fair return from absence latest; handicap debut

runners.name: Campenaerts
runners.spotlight: Won two 5f events (including on soft) from final three handicaps for Archie Watson; well held in two runs for new yard and may need more weight off his back; first 6f run since debut.
runners.comment: Won two 5f events from final 3 handicaps for previous trainer; well held for new one

runners.name: Acclaimed Freedom
runners.spotlight: Some promise on 6f debut at Ascot (soft) and didn't need to come on a ton to win a 7f Chelmsford AW novice next time; has not looked ahead of his mark in two AW handicaps this year but perhaps he'll do better back at 6f on soft turf.
runners.comment: Some 6f promise on soft, then won over 7f on AW; not looked ahead of his mark in handicaps
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Cloudbuster
runners.spotlight: Can lead; has form at both sprint trips but her win came in a 6f Chepstow maiden (good); had the widest draw to overcome when finishing last of nine on Lingfield AW in May and has more going for her in this lower grade back at 6f; first-time cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Sole win in 6f Chepstow maiden; had excuses on AW return; down in grade back on turf

runners.name: Exhibitioning
runners.spotlight: Best form on the second of four starts, when runner-up in a C&D novice last August; beaten a long way on her handicap debut at Haydock in October, when the 7f trip and/or the good to soft ground might have counted against her; off since and some guesswork needed.
runners.comment: Best form in the 2nd of 4 starts, in a C&D novice; off since October; guesswork involved

runners.name: Got The Booty
runners.spotlight: Two narrow 6f defeats on the AW either side of a winter break, the latest at Southwell against a subsequent winner, but she didn't do as well there next time and the return to turf didn't pay dividends at Catterick since; goes into handicap company with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Two narrow 6f defeats on AW either side of a break; not so good on last two starts

runners.name: From The Hip
runners.spotlight: Well positioned when winning final two juvenile starts at 6f on Polytrack; entitled to need the run on his recent comeback at Haydock (6f, soft) and open to some improvement regardless of what the weather does.
runners.comment: Ended 2yo season with two 6f wins on AW; creditable return; open to some improvement

runners.name: Woolisle
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo form in the fifth of five handicap starts, when a ready winner over 5f at Nottingham (soft) last October and emerged with credit from her return, coming clear with an improved rival at Haydock (6f, soft; first two well clear) recently; up 2lb but one to fear if conditions deteriorate.
runners.comment: Suited by soft ground; ready 5f winner on final 2yo start; good return in May (6f, soft)
runners.owner: Paul G Jacobs And Wetumpka Racing

runners.name: Grey Horizon
runners.spotlight: Hardy customer who has already run 23 times and was gaining his sixth win when successful at Brighton (6f, good to firm; has won on soft) ten days ago; back up 5lb but not weighted out of it; hereabouts.
runners.comment: Has run 23 times; won for the sixth time at Brighton latest; back up 5lb; thereabouts

runners.name: Flying Squad
runners.spotlight: Unable to sustain effort upped to 1m on Kempton AW in April and was always well adrift on final qualifying run there when back over 7f; unknown quantity over 6f with questions about how the ground wil be riding but, as a newcomer to handicaps for a leading yard, he has to be respected.
runners.comment: Signs of ability over 1m on AW; always behind back at 7f latest; handicap debut over 6f

runners.name: Unionville
runners.spotlight: Some turf promise in Ireland; has done better on AW for this yard, finishing a close second at Kempton on handicap debut, then dead-heating for a Chelmsford maiden (also 6f); never involved at Kempton in April but capable of better down in grade.
runners.comment: Improved form at 6f on AW for this yard; up against it latest but now down in grade

runners.name: Oceti
runners.spotlight: All three runs on good ground; had four subsequent winners ahead of him when a front-running eighth of 25 at Newbury (1m) on reappearance but he didn't run to the same level back over 7f there next time and it's hard to know what to expect on the drop in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Best form over 1m; lesser run back at 7f latest; trip and ground query now dropped to 6f

races.race_name: O'Malley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Glorious Game
runners.spotlight: £85,000 yearling by Pinatubo; eighth foal; closely related to winners Feliciana De Vega (7f-1m1f including 2yo/AW/Group 3; RPR 111) and Order Of Malta (1m2f; 82), half-sister to three winners including Matsuri (1m AW 2yo/1m2f turf; 117); dam 1m-9.4f winner (including Listed; 111); tongue tied for this debut; one to note for trainer whose 2yos are making a splash.
runners.comment: £85,000 yearling by Pinatubo; closely related to Feliciana De Vega (7f-1m1f wins; RPR 111)

runners.name: Spirit Of Glory
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; Cotai Glory half-sister to winners Starlight Time (7f/1m 2yo; RPR 86) and Be Glorious (6f AW 2yo; 73); dam 1m winner (including Listed; 105), half-sister to UAE 1,000 Guineas winner Lovely Pass, out of 1m Group 2 winner; from powerful yard and strength in the betting must be monitored.
runners.comment: Cotai Glory filly; from powerful yard and strength in the betting must be monitored

runners.name: Bymiddaytomorrow
runners.spotlight: 90,000gns yearling by Mehmas; sister to 6f/7f 2yo scorer The Waco Kid (including Group 3; RPR 106); late foal; she's the only one in this field with racing experience, albeit not setting the world alight with her last of six in C&D novice (good to firm; 20-1) five weeks ago.
runners.comment: The only one with racing experience, albeit last of six in C&D novice (good to firm; 20-1)

runners.name: Desert Symphony
runners.spotlight: 72,000euros yearling by Ghaiyyath; tenth foal; closely related to 6f 2yo winner Bay Of Plenty (RPR 97), half-sister to five winners including Glesga Gal (7f AW; 100) and Vivianite (1m; 99); dam Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner (98); her trainer's first 2yo runner of the season.
runners.comment: 72,000euros yearling by Ghaiyyath; her trainer's first 2yo runner of the season

runners.name: Starburster
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros foal, £125,000 yearling by Starman; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Electric Waves (5f 2yo Group 3), Appealing (useful 6f-1m) and Tut (7f 2yo), out of 1m-winning half-sister to Italian 1,000 Guineas third Baranja; connections run another six-figure purchase in Motown Filly.
runners.comment: £125,000 yearling by Starman; connections run another six-figure purchase in Motown Filly

runners.name: Courage Best
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; seventh foal; Supremacy half-sister to winners Punita Arora (5f 2yo/5.5f Listed; RPR 99), Marching Mac (5f-6f; 90) and Sangria (1m AW; 77); dam 1m winner (75), half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Muthabara and Group-placed 7f winner Mustadeem; trainer (runs two) had 2yo win in April.
runners.comment: 40,000gns yearling; half-sister to Punita Arora (5f 2yo/5.5f Listed winner; RPR 99)

runners.name: Motown Filly
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns yearling by Cotai Glory; ninth foal; half-sister to six winners including The Mackem Bullet (6f-1m including 2yo/US Grade 2; RPR 111), She's So Nice (6f including 2yo/US; 91) and Kick On Girl (1m 2yo; 81); dam 6f 2yo winner (80), half-sister to 2,000 Guineas third Stubbs Art; no 2yo win yet this term for the yard (runs two in this) but she needs a market check.
runners.comment: 100,000gns yearling by Cotai Glory; half-sister to The Mackem Bullet (6f-1m wins; RPR 111)

runners.name: Perfect Hope
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; Perfect Power half-sister to French/UAE 5.5f-7f winner Itsatenfromlen (including 2yo/dirt; RPR 92); dam runner-up 8.6f AW 2yo (78), half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Kilmah, out of 1m Listed winner; early days for the stable's 2yos.
runners.comment: Perfect Power half-sister to 5.5f-7f winner Itsatenfromlen (including 2yo/dirt; RPR 92)

runners.name: Leading Raya
runners.spotlight: By Nando Parrado; second foal; dam Swedish 1m winner, half-sister to winners Miss Marble (useful 1m) and Master Diver (7f), out of Group-placed 6f AW winning half-sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo winner Rockaway Valley; others are a bit more striking on paper.
runners.comment: By Nando Parrado; dam Swedish 1m winner; others are a bit more striking on paper

runners.name: Lola De Valence
runners.spotlight: Third foal; Havana Grey half-sister to 7f winner Back In Black (including 2yo; RPR 101); dam 7f 2yo winner (78), half-sister to 1m Listed winner Awesometank, out of 6f 2yo winner; trainer also runs Courage Best.
runners.comment: Havana Grey half-sister to 7f winner Back In Black (including 2yo; RPR 101); yard runs two

runners.name: Phillipa
runners.spotlight: Third foal; Dark Angel half-sister to 1m AW 2yo winner Beckon (RPR 67); dam once-raced sister to smart/prolific 6f-1m2f winner Muntadab, closely related to Coventry Stakes winner Nando Parrado, out of useful Argentinian 6f-1m winner; stable's 2yos yet to place this term but this one needs a second look.
runners.comment: By Dark Angel; stable's 2yos yet to place this term but this one needs a second look

runners.name: Roxelina
runners.spotlight: 170,000gns foal by Havana Grey; sister to 5f-1m winner Holguin (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 113), half-sister to 5f/6f winner Captain's Bar (including 2yo; 79); dam 1m AW winner (71); 6-5 for a C&D novice five weeks ago but got on edge and reared over at the stalls, so was withdrawn; hopefully keeps her cool this time and clearly very much one to note.
runners.comment: 170,000gns foal by Havana Grey; 6-5 for C&D novice five weeks ago but reared and withdrawn

races.race_name: Hunter Plant Hire/MC International Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Bayaann
runners.spotlight: 410,000gns yearling by Too Darn Hot; made a smooth start with 7f maiden win on Lingfield AW in November and built substantially on that when second in a novice at Newmarket (1m, good) last month; that sets a high standard.
runners.comment: Won sole 2yo race (7f, AW); second at Newmarket (1m, good) last month sets a high standard

runners.name: Eljowhary
runners.spotlight: 140,000gns yearling; mid-division last month on AW at Newcastle (7f) and Southwell (1m), which showed significant ability but two of today's rivals have better form; a hood goes on.
runners.comment: Mid-division on AW at 7f/1m in May; two of today's rivals have better form; a hood goes on

runners.name: Storming Point
runners.spotlight: Dam was Group and Listed-placed at up to 1m1f in France; fair form when placed at Newbury (extended 6f, soft) in October and Kempton (7f, AW) 12 days ago; could still do better and has a strong place chance.
runners.comment: Placed at Newbury (extended 6f, soft) in October and Kempton (7f, AW) 12 days ago

runners.name: Screen Actor
runners.spotlight: $100,000 yearling, 40,000gns breeze-up 2yo by Twirling Candy; fourth foal; dam US Graded stakes-placed 1m/8.5f turf/dirt winner, half-sister to UAE 1m Group 2 winner Barbecue Eddie; probably best watched on debut; refused to enter stalls and withdrawn from last month's intended debut (28-1 at the time).
runners.comment: $100,000 yearling, 40,000gns breeze-up 2yo by Twirling Candy; probably best watched

runners.name: Florizelle
runners.spotlight: Improved when 9l sixth of 11 in novice at Nottingham (1m, good to soft; 300-1) three weeks ago but that's still modest form.
runners.comment: Improved when 9l sixth of 11 at Nottingham (1m, good to soft; 300-1) but it's modest form

runners.name: Sonbelievable
runners.spotlight: 9,000gns foal, £28,000 yearling; sixth foal; Mayson half-brother to winners Validated (GB 7f/HK 1m; RPR 99) and Credible (6f 2yo; 79); dam 6f AW 2yo winner (74), half-sister to winners Infallible (7f Group 3, dam of 1m Group 1 winner Veracious) and Remarkable (6f Listed); probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: £28,000 yearling; Mayson half-brother to two winners, notably Validated (7f/1m; RPR 99)

runners.name: Lean d'Aislingean
runners.spotlight: 14-1, made most when well-beaten ninth of 11 in a novice at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) three weeks ago.
runners.comment: 14-1, made most when well-beaten ninth of 11 in a novice at Nottingham (1m, good to soft)

runners.name: Heir Of Ambition
runners.spotlight: 66-1, slowly away and checked at the start when tailed off in a novice at Southwell (1m, AW) in April.
runners.comment: 66-1, slowly away and checked when tailed off in a novice at Southwell (1m, AW) in April

runners.name: Paean Of Appin
runners.spotlight: Finished last in Newbury maidens over 6f (good to soft; 25-1) in September and 1m2f (good; 125-1) in April; should do better in due course but has no claims at the moment.
runners.comment: Finished last in Newbury maidens over 6f in September and 1m2f in April

runners.name: Minerality
runners.spotlight: Well related but showed low-level form over 1m on AW at Lingfield (50-1) and Wolverhampton (125-1) recently.
runners.comment: Well related but low-level form at 1m on AW at Lingfield (50-1) and Wolverhampton (125-1)

runners.name: Love Me Bae
runners.spotlight: 150-1, tailed-off last of nine in novice at Chelmsford (1m, AW) in December when trained by Alice Haynes.
runners.comment: 150-1, tailed-off last at Chelmsford (1m, AW) in December when trained by Alice Haynes

races.race_name: Hunter Plant Hire/MC International Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Kahin
runners.spotlight: 240,000gns foal by Kingman; too green over C&D (good to firm) on debut early last month but he has scope and did much better when a close fourth of 12 as favourite in maiden at Southwell (1m, AW) 17 days later, always prominent; could still do with calming down a bit and also hung left in the closing stages last time, but he's a major contender again.
runners.comment: Far from the finished article but close 4th at Southwell (1m, AW) makes him a major player

runners.name: Atlas Mountain
runners.spotlight: Three starts, best effort when a close and closing third of 11 from off the pace in novice at Nottingham (1m, good to soft; 20-1) three weeks ago; retains potential and should be placed at the least.
runners.comment: A close and closing third of 11 at Nottingham (1m, good to soft; 20-1) three weeks ago

runners.name: Cockade
runners.spotlight: Late foal, by Pinatubo from a notable family with stamina; second favourite when fourth of ten in a hot novice at Kempton (1m, AW) in December, making most; should improve and have a good shout in this race.
runners.comment: Second favourite when 4th of 10 in hot novice at Kempton (1m, AW) in December, making most

runners.name: Sound Gloria
runners.spotlight: Ran twice at Southwell (1m, AW) and finished closer last time but was nevertheless beaten 14l at 50-1; late foal who looks bred for much further.
runners.comment: Twice well beaten at Southwell (1m, AW); late foal who looks bred for much further

runners.name: El Presidente
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; Havana Grey half-brother to winners Durdle Door (6f/7f AW; RPR 85), Zoukster (6f/7f; 74) and Princess Salamah (8.6f AW 2yo; 72); dam 6f winner (97), half-sister to 8.5f 2yo Grade 1 winner Square Eddie; market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: 9th foal; Havana Grey half-brother to three winners, one pretty useful; dam 6f winner (97)

runners.name: Champagne Beau
runners.spotlight: By Frontiersman; second foal; dam 1m2f turf/1m3f AW winner (RPR 64), out of 1m3f-1m4f winning half-sister to useful 6f winners Kellys Eye and El Manati; late foal; sire and dam seem to point to longer trips.
runners.comment: Late foal; sire and dam seem to point to longer trips

runners.name: Next Guilian
runners.spotlight: By Guiliani; eighth foal; half-brother to six winners including Next Mine (1m 2yo/Italian Group 2; RPR 115) and Not Mine (Listed-placed 9.5f; 110); dam German 1m/1m1f winner (94), half-sister to German Derby winner Next Desert; 16-1, about 8l third of nine in maiden at Cologne (1m1f, soft) last November, hooded for new connections but he clearly possesses significant ability.
runners.comment: 16-1, about 8l third of nine at Cologne (1m1f, soft) last November, hooded for new yard

runners.name: Renovator
runners.spotlight: 125-1, no show when last of 12 in maiden at Southwell (1m, AW) 18 days ago, a long way behind Kahin.
runners.comment: 125-1, no show when last of 12 in maiden at Southwell (1m, AW) 18 days ago

runners.name: Baareq
runners.spotlight: 160,000gns yearling by Bated Breath; third foal; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 81), half-sister to winners Viadera (US 1m Grade 1), Sacred Bridge (6f 2yo Group 3) and Cross Baton (1m2f Listed); gelded newcomer (carrying notable colours) aged four who needs a market check.
runners.comment: 160,000gns yearling by Bated Breath; gelded newcomer (carrying notable colours) aged four

runners.name: Star Baby
runners.spotlight: 8l sixth of 12 in maiden at Kempton (1m, AW) in April but she was second favourite and made some late headway; should do better but some others have accomplished much more.
runners.comment: 8l sixth of 12 at Kempton (1m, AW) in April, second favourite and making some late headway

runners.name: Peace And Quiet
runners.spotlight: Well related but not seen until three weeks ago when 200-1 and beaten about 10l in seventh of 11 for novice at Nottingham (1m, good to soft); should build on that but it was modest form.
runners.comment: 200-1, about 10l seventh of 11 for novice at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) last month

races.race_name: Install Electrical Contractors Limited Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBplus Race)

runners.name: Cyrano De Bergerac
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling by Palace Pier; second foal; dam well-related 1m4f AW winner (RPR 78); 7-4 when chasing home an impressive 1-2 favourite in eight-runner maiden at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) six weeks ago, outpaced before grabbing second on the line; it wasn't great form behind him but he looks sure to improve and should go well in this race.
runners.comment: Chased home impressive favourite at Lingfield (1m2f, AW), outpaced before grabbing second

runners.name: Cuff It
runners.spotlight: £55,000 yearling by City Light; 22-1 when 8l fifth of ten in novice at Newmarket (1m, good) last month, coming from the back for an encouraging debut; the runner-up was Bayaann who is declared for today's 6.09; it looks as if 1m2f will help and he's one of the top contenders on form.
runners.comment: 22-1 at Newmarket (1m, good) last month, coming from the back for an encouraging debut 5th

runners.name: Indhaar
runners.spotlight: By Ghaiyyath; closely related to 6f turf 2yo/7f AW winner Red Sand (RPR 89), half-brother to three winners including Red Mist (Listed-placed 7f/1m including 2yo; 107); dam Listed-placed 7f/1m winner (103) from family of top middle-distance filly Islington; withdrawn lame at Newbury (1m2f) over three weeks ago; one to note and the market should give useful clues.
runners.comment: By Ghaiyyath from family of top middle-distance filly Islington; one to note

runners.name: Steamroller
runners.spotlight: 200,000euros yearling by Too Darn Hot; well bred, including as half-brother to Queen Of The Pride (1m2f-1m4f winner; RPR 115); 50-1, raced freely early on when 6l sixth of nine in maiden at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month, never dangerous but showing promise; could be interesting.
runners.comment: Well bred; 50-1, raced freely when sixth of nine at Newbury (1m2f, good) but some promise

runners.name: Zachary Hickes
runners.spotlight: 105,000gns yearling; 25-1 for novice at Kempton (1m, AW) in December 2024 but showed plenty of ability staying on well from towards rear into fourth of 13; big player judged on that, but with the cautionary note that he's not been seen since; gelded last September; needs a market check.
runners.comment: Big player judged on debut fourth (1m, AW) in December 2024 but he's not been seen since

runners.name: Phantom Recon
runners.spotlight: 340,000euros yearling by Teofilo; closely related to Group-placed French/German 10.5f-12.2f winner Tres Rock Women (including AW; RPR 110), half-brother to French 1m4f winner Tiger Rock; dam 1m4f winner (including Listed; 98), sister to Listed-placed 1m2f/1m3f winner; withdrawn with an abscess at Newbury (1m2f) over three weeks ago; yard runs two; needs a close look.
runners.comment: 340,000euros yearling by Teofilo; closely related to a Group-placed 10.5f-12.2f winner

runners.name: Avarona
runners.spotlight: 10-1, slowly away and only brief headway when 7l seventh of eight in a fillies' novice at Newbury (1m, soft) last October which was dominated by two stablemates; capable of better but looks bred for further still.
runners.comment: Capable of better than she showed at Newbury (1m, soft) as 2yo but bred for further still
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Sevenkingsmustdie
runners.spotlight: Originally cost 425,000gns as a yearling; failed to settle when well beaten in bumpers at Warwick (hooded) and Haydock (tailed off) this spring; now switches to Flat rules with the hood restored.
runners.comment: Failed to settle when well beaten in two bumpers this spring, hooded on first occasions

runners.name: Asterial
runners.spotlight: 750,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; second foal; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 89), closely related to 6f Listed winner Naadirr, half-sister to winners including Dubai Mile (1m2f 2yo Group 1); for powerful owners and for stable which runs two, the market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: 750,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; second foal; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 89); needs a check

runners.name: All Of My Heart
runners.spotlight: 100-1, pulled hard and made most when behind in a bumper at Ludlow (2m, good to soft) in December.
runners.comment: 100-1, pulled hard and made most when behind in a bumper at Ludlow in December

runners.name: Zynak
runners.spotlight: 100,000euros yearling by Zarak; late foal; 66-1, never dangerous when fading into last of nine at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month, 6l behind Steamroller.
runners.comment: 66-1, faded into last of nine at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month, 6l behind Steamroller

runners.name: Zurna
runners.spotlight: Well related but 200-1 when weakening into ninth of ten for a novice at Nottingham (1m2f, good to soft) three weeks ago.
runners.comment: 200-1 when weakening into ninth of ten for a novice at Nottingham (1m2f, good to soft)

runners.name: Virtue Diligence
runners.spotlight: 125-1, slow-starting ninth of 11 in novice at Kempton (1m, AW) in September, so has major work to do.
runners.comment: 125-1, slow-starting ninth of 11 in novice at Kempton (1m, AW) in September

runners.name: Strong Man Tom
runners.spotlight: Remote in two bumpers and beaten about 37l in two 1m2f Chepstow maidens.
runners.comment: Remote in two bumpers and beaten about 37l in two 1m2f Chepstow maidens

runners.name: Eshowe
runners.spotlight: 80-1 and hooded when last of 12 in maiden at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in December, always behind; no hood today but he needs a transformation.
runners.comment: 80-1 and hooded when last of 12 in maiden at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in December

runners.name: Prophet Squeeze
runners.spotlight: A lowly rated maiden over jumps who tailed off on Flat debut at Chepstow (1m2f) ten days ago; hooded on last eight starts.
runners.comment: A lowly rated maiden over jumps who tailed off on Flat debut (1m2f) ten days ago

races.race_name: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Clearpoint
runners.spotlight: Superb run of AW form at 5f in the autumn, winning three in a row, two from the front; has remained in form since but he couldn't sustain his effort back on turf at Goodwood last time and needs to show he can reproduce his sand form on grass.
runners.comment: Excellent spell over 5f on AW but couldn't reproduce it back on turf at Goodwood latest

runners.name: Regal Envoy
runners.spotlight: Useful strike-rate and 4-6 over 5f for Oisin Murphy; can lead; versatile regarding tracks but he won his first two C&D starts (including this race when 3lb higher) and gave the principals too much rope back here in May, albeit he still found only an improved one too good; best form on good or quicker ground and rarely asked to tackle slower surfaces.
runners.comment: 2-3 over C&D and won this when 3lb higher last year; best form on good or quicker ground

runners.name: Society Kiss
runners.spotlight: Winning 5f debut (good to firm; equalled track record) for Ralph Beckett then mid-division in Group 2 Queen Mary at Royal Ascot; first run since when a never-nearer close third in a Southwell AW novice, not getting the best of runs, to one who ran well in the 3yo Dash at Epsom last week; sire and dam both handled ground slower than good; has potential on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Winning 5f debut as 2yo for R Beckett; promising return from absence for new yard

runners.name: Trefor
runners.spotlight: Nearly all wins over 6f, including two on this course, but speed wasn't in short supply when he won over about 5f at York (good to firm) last August; has had a marked preference for good or quicker ground up to now, though, so further rain would spell danger.
runners.comment: Two course wins at 6f but has the speed for 5f; all best form on good or quicker going
runners.owner: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner

runners.name: Gaeli
runners.spotlight: Five sprint wins on turf in Italy on ground ranging from good to heavy; smart AW form when winning on stable debut at Southwell (5f) in March but not as effective off 6lb higher since, on that track and over this C&D; needs extra; first-time cheekpieces replace blinkers.
runners.comment: Smart AW form over 5f on yard debut; not so effective since, including over C&D latest

runners.name: Bassenthwaite
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who dropped out over the new 6f trip on Newcastle AW when last seen in January; had won two of his five 5f starts previously, producing a major career best when a wide-margin winner on Wolverhampton AW last November (made all), but a sole run on soft met with failure; another with a surface query.
runners.comment: 2-5 over 5f, most recently on AW in November; dropped out over 6f latest; ground query

runners.name: Roach Power
runners.spotlight: Five wins in 2025 (all at 5f; on good, heavy and soft ground); had trip and draw excuses for first two runs this year but he'd shot his bolt over a furlong out at Goodwood last time out; might do better back on a more conventional track and the change in the weather brings him into it.
runners.comment: Five wins over 5f in 2025, including on heavy; minor form this year but revival possible

runners.name: Marching Mac
runners.spotlight: Multiple wins at sprint trips; they were all in lower grades than today but he's not badly treated, even from 3lb wrong, and the slower the ground the better his chance against some who prefer good ground.
runners.comment: Multiple wins in lower grades than this; 3lb wrong but the more rain the better

runners.name: Rocking Ends
runners.spotlight: Acts well on this track and he made it 2-3 over C&D (both on good to firm) last August; had a spell in Bahrain over the winter but although he might well come on for his first run back in Britain, at Yarmouth last month, he would have more to prove if the ground is slower than good.
runners.comment: Acts well on this track; made it 2-3 over C&D last August; best on good or quicker ground

races.race_name: Heat Your Home With Alpha Classified Stakes

runners.name: Qaaeadd
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden with the odd piece of modest form (good to firm and AW, including 9.4f) which gives him a chance.
runners.comment: The odd piece of modest form (good to firm and AW, including 9.4f) gives him a chance

runners.name: Guinness Lad
runners.spotlight: 0-18 before he won a C&D handicap (good to firm) last Monday, coming from the rear; well beaten in his hurdle races on softer than good but has not encountered it on Flat and having some recent form is respected.
runners.comment: 0-18 before he won a C&D handicap (good to firm) last Monday, coming from the rear

runners.name: Dubai Harbour
runners.spotlight: Both wins on Lingfield AW over 1m2f; fifth of 13 last time in a 7.6f handicap at Lingfield (good) was the best he's managed in four attempts on turf and he needs further than that, but there's a question mark about this trip.
runners.comment: Needs further than the 7.6f of latest start but there's a question mark about this trip

runners.name: Gearing's Point
runners.spotlight: All four wins at about this trip in 2023; does not look the force of old (missed most of last year) but her second in C&D handicap on penultimate start gives her a shot; not proven on softer than good.
runners.comment: C&D handicap second on penultimate start gives her a shot; not proven on softer than good

runners.name: Late Claim
runners.spotlight: Won handicap debut at Lingfield (1m5f, AW) in December and runner-up three times since but her form has subsided; minor honours in two bumpers last summer at start of her career have been her only races on turf.
runners.comment: AW form has subsided; minor honours in bumpers last summer have been her only turf races

runners.name: Nymphaea
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden who's found her level since cheekpieces went on, mostly on AW but she wasn't far away in a 1m4f Beverley handicap on good to soft either; each-way player.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden who's found her level since cheekpieces went on; each-way player

runners.name: Lawn Ranger
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is often a zestful front-runner; no win since August 2023 but 5-15 at Windsor overall and, surprisingly, his last race here was that latest win; close second on Kempton AW (1m4f) on recent reappearance also returns him to calculations and he's won on a wide variety of ground.
runners.comment: Veteran who is often front-runner; 5-15 here and his last race here was latest win (2023)

runners.name: Dream Of Ithaca
runners.spotlight: Raced freely and faded into fifth of 14 in C&D handicap (good to firm) last time, but that sixth start confirmed that she has a little ability.
runners.comment: Raced freely and faded into fifth of 14 in C&D handicap (good to firm) last time

runners.name: Panthere Noir
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden; bettered low-key stable debut and belied 50-1 odds when third of 14 in C&D handicap (good to firm; refitted cheekpieces) 16 days ago, making most; more to prove if the ground is on softer than good but that latest run is not his only piece of significant form.
runners.comment: Third in C&D handicap latest; more to prove on softer than good but he's considered

runners.name: Arenas Del Tiempo
runners.spotlight: Took an AW race last August but no turf win since 2021; questionable record on softer than good and she does not look the percentage call.
runners.comment: AW win last August but no turf win since 2021; questionable record on softer than good
runners.owner: D Edmonston, T Helliwell & S J Holden

runners.name: Fletcher
runners.spotlight: Fourth on seasonal/handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) was his best result but a heavy defeat in 11.6f handicap at Lingfield (good to firm) dents any appeal.
runners.comment: Heavy defeat in 11.6f handicap at Lingfield (good to firm) last time dents any appeal

runners.name: Clough
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form (like stablemate Lilykoy) after a maiden and two novice races, the last of which was over 1m2f at this track; downgraded, stamina in the family and he's tongue tied and blinkered, both for the first time.
runners.comment: Downgraded, stamina in family and he's tongue tied and blinkered, both for the first time
runners.owner: Matt Broughton, Chris Hughes & D Cunha

runners.name: Majestic Leo
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden whose 2025 and 2026 form (including in handicaps here on last two starts) give him major work to do.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden whose 2025 and 2026 form give him major work to do

runners.name: Nala The Lioness
runners.spotlight: Runner-up four times for Karl Burke (including in three bumpers, one on good to soft) but lost her form with that yard and, following a 400-day absence, there's been no sign of regaining it this year; had wind surgery after latest start and returns to turf for first time since her bumper days, so if she returned to form there would be clear reasons.
runners.comment: Wind surgery after latest start; returns to turf for first time since her bumper days

runners.name: Scammer
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form in five starts over 7f-1m6f on the AW, including a classified and handicap with other headgear; visor is given a go this time.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form in five starts over 7f-1m6f on the AW, including with other headgear

runners.name: Lilykoy
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form (like stablemate Clough) as 2yo in two runs over 7f and one at 6f; there's a very different trip for this reappearance at a much lower level and there seems to be stacks of stamina on the dam's side, so she needs a second look in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Stacks of stamina on the dam's side, so she needs a second look in first-time headgear

races.race_name: Network Airline Services Handicap

runners.name: Buckland Belle
runners.spotlight: 0-7 on the AW and it was her turf debut when edging home over C&D two weeks ago, keeping on to spring 25-1 surprise; only raised 3lb and the third won on Saturday, so definite chance if handling this different surface; Oisin Murphy takes over.
runners.comment: Recent C&D winner raised only 3lb; definite chance if handling this different surface

runners.name: Starakova
runners.spotlight: Unraced beyond 7f over which she finished well off a strong pace to get off the mark at Wolverhampton; raised 6lb but she's a last-time-out winner taking on a lot of horses with much to prove.
runners.comment: A last-time-out winner taking on a lot of horses with much to prove
runners.owner: Howard Bolly Sutton Molony Weller Cowles

runners.name: Diamond Ali
runners.spotlight: Last three handicap runs nothing to crow about but what offers hope is that his two respectable shows in Leicester handicaps (6f-7f) were on good to soft and soft; should stay this far on breeding so not dismissed.
runners.comment: Last three handicap runs nothing to crow about but returning to softer ground a plus

runners.name: Pepper Fizz
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who failed to progress last season and this campaign hasn't got off to the best of starts, finishing sixth of eight here over 1m and tailed off 16 days ago over 1m3f (first-time cheekpieces).
runners.comment: This season she's finished sixth of eight and been tailed off

runners.name: Sorted
runners.spotlight: Has improved for the switch to handicaps, placed in the last two at Wolverhampton (8.6f; finished upside Nautical Sky in the latest); had a while off but needs respecting.
runners.comment: Has improved for the switch to handicaps, placed in the last two at Wolverhampton

runners.name: Ballon Rouge
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in both her handicaps, on the AW at 1m4f and 1m2f; on the plus side, her best maiden run was on good to soft and cheekpieces have made a big difference to her stablemate Zoustar Dreams.
runners.comment: Quiet in handicaps but this ground might suit and now goes in cheekpieces

runners.name: Blue Jammin
runners.spotlight: Won a weak soft-ground Chepstow 1m nursery in September off 3lb lower; the ground was fast when down the field here 16 days ago and he's of greater interest under these conditions.
runners.comment: Quiet on fast ground here on comeback but should be happier on this surface

runners.name: Heated Moment
runners.spotlight: 0-5 for Ollie Sangster and it's been a quiet start for this yard in 1m2f handicaps; now blinkered.
runners.comment: 0-5 for Ollie Sangster and it's been a quiet start for this yard in 1m2f handicaps

runners.name: Back To Black
runners.spotlight: Heavy defeats in these colours for William Haggas (over 5f-6f) and was beaten 22l on stable/handicap debut upped to 7f at Lingfield (good; 28-1); hard to warm up.
runners.comment: Well-bred filly but hard to warm to on what she's shown thus far, including a handicap

runners.name: Curtain Caller
runners.spotlight: Minor form in her qualifying races (7f-1m) and not even this low a mark looks that generous; perhaps cheekpieces can spark something.
runners.comment: Low-level form ahead of this handicap debut; perhaps cheekpieces can spark something

runners.name: My Old Mate
runners.spotlight: Only eighth behind Buckland Belle over C&D (good to firm) two weeks ago but was only about 4l off the winner and that represented some improvement.
runners.comment: Only eighth to Buckland Belle here 14 days ago but he wasn't beaten all that far

runners.name: Nautical Sky
runners.spotlight: Insufficient pace in 6f maidens but ran better upped to 8.6f on handicap debut at Wolverhampton, albeit no match for the 6l winner; may have more to offer.
runners.comment: Insufficient pace in 6f maidens but ran better upped to 8.6f on handicap debut

runners.name: Summer Oasis
runners.spotlight: Failed to offer much in her three outings last autumn and same again on April's handicap debut (7f AW; 100-1).
runners.comment: Failed to offer much in her three outings last autumn and same again on handicap debut

runners.name: King's Courtier
runners.spotlight: Beaten five times, easily the best run when fourth of eight in an AW maiden (9.4f); finished tailed off on handicap debut latest (1m2f) and blinkers are now turned to.
runners.comment: Finished tailed off on handicap debut latest (1m2f) and blinkers are now turned to

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino Bradford Apprentice Handicap (Apprentice Training Race - Racing Excellence Series)

runners.name: Forever Noah
runners.spotlight: Races mainly on the AW; not that easy to win with (record of 1-21); in fair form when last out, finishing runner-up in three of his last four races (all at 6f) having won over 6f at Chelmsford in February; first turf run since May last year when fifth of six at Brighton (6f, good to firm).
runners.comment: 1-21 on AW; well beaten last turf run but in fair form on AW; not easy to win with

runners.name: Komorkis
runners.spotlight: Ex-Richard Fahey; two fair runs for this yard this season, including when fourth off 2lb higher at Carlisle last time (5f, good); chance.
runners.comment: Ex-Richard Fahey; two fair runs for this yard, including when 4th last time; chance

runners.name: Isla Bella
runners.spotlight: Only win was a Brighton maiden in May 2024 (6f, good); best recent run was when third at Wolverhampton in April but has been mainly disappointing this year; others stronger.
runners.comment: Only win was a turf maiden in 2024; mainly disappointing this year; others look stronger

runners.name: Lady Bouquet
runners.spotlight: Catterick winner last season (5f, good to soft); not particularly consistent, and although she ran well when third at Catterick in May when back after a break, she was well below that form at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last time.
runners.comment: Not particularly consistent; good 3rd at Catterick two runs back, but below that last time

runners.name: Knicks
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for more than two years and not at his best on last two starts (fifth over C&D on good to soft last time); on a good mark, but a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Last win came in May 2024; not been at his best this season and has a bit to prove

runners.name: Thornaby Pearl
runners.spotlight: Hamilton winner last August (5f, good); mixed form this season; could go well if it rains as best efforts on good going or softer
runners.comment: Hamilton win in August; mixed form this term; best form on good or softer so needs rain

runners.name: Reidh
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 7f for Richard Fahey in 2024; not added to that tally since, and has been below-par on both starts for new yard (7f); takes a marked drop in trip.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins for Richard Fahey in 2024; below-par for new yard; marked drop in trip

races.race_name: Racing TV EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB Race)

runners.name: Fern Clyde
runners.spotlight: Improved on her debut run when in the frame in two 6f novice events at Ripon last month; each-way chance.
runners.comment: In the frame in two 6f novice events at Ripon last month; each-way chance

runners.name: Cousin Rachel
runners.spotlight: Home-bred by Postponed; third foal; closely related to two smart winners at up to 1m1f; dam French Group-placed 7.5f AW 2yo winner; may need further in time, but one to monitor in the betting.
runners.comment: By Postponed; third foal; closely related to two smart winners; interesting newcomer
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Flower Youmzain
runners.spotlight: By Ardad; dam unraced half-sister to two bumper winners (one a 1m2f-2m Flat winner); stables juveniles going well; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Dam half-sister to two bumper winners; yard's juveniles going well; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Bayside Way
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros yearling who was bought for £18,000 at the breeze-up; half-sister to two winners; dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (98); wears a hood on debut.
runners.comment: £18,000 breeze-up buy; dam Listed-placed 2yo winner; wears a hood on debut

runners.name: Black Cuillin
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal of a 7f/1m winner; only beaten 2.5l when last of four in a 5f maiden here in April (good; 33-1) but that form hasn't worked out particularly well.
runners.comment: Beaten 2.5l when last of four in a 5f maiden here in April; that form nothing special

runners.name: Mohangel
runners.spotlight: 5,000gns yearling by Mohaather; fourth foal; dam placed 1m AW/1m2f; perhaps best watched on debut.
runners.comment: 5,000gns yearling; fourth foal; dam placed 1m AW/1m2f; perhaps best watched on debut.

runners.name: Consultation
runners.spotlight: Home-bred by Twilight Son; 14-1 when 2.5l fifth of six in a Wolverhampton maiden on debut last month (6f; fourth has won since); should improve; interesting.
runners.comment: By Twilight Son; 14-1 when 5th of six on AW debut last month; should improve; interesting

runners.name: Pearl Grace
runners.spotlight: 35,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to two winners; dam 7f-1m4f winner including the Irish 1,000 Guineas and Yorkshire Oaks; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 35,000gns yearling; dam won Irish 1,000 Guineas and Yorkshire Oaks; interesting newcomer.

runners.name: Shaqra Diamond
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling by Space Traveller; sixth foal; half-sister to four winners; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: 20,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to four winners; worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Bladey Lady
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form when in the frame in novice events at Beverley (5f, good to firm) and Ripon (6f, good); likely nursery sort.
runners.comment: Ordinary form in two novice events over 5f/6f and is a likely sort for nurseries

runners.name: Khazamh
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns breeze-up buy; well beaten last of nine when an 11-1 shot for a Redcar maiden last month; more needed.
runners.comment: 20,000gns 2yo; well beaten last of nine for a Redcar maiden last month; more needed.

races.race_name: Fathers Day Family Day Sunday 21st June Handicap (GBBplus Race)

runners.name: Analogical
runners.spotlight: Progressive; made all to win a five-runner handicap here last July (1m4f, good; blinkers on for first time) and won his second race of the current season when making all at Catterick last time (1m4f, good); on a career-high mark (up 2lb for last win), but should go well again.
runners.comment: Progressive; C&D winner in 2025; two wins this term, latest 18 days ago; up 2lb; chance

runners.name: Say What You See
runners.spotlight: Passed the post in front on three occasions last season (1m2f/1m4f, AW, good to soft) but was disqualified on third occasion due to banned substance; two fair runs this year, latest a 2nd on AW; ideally suited by some give in the ground.
runners.comment: Stays 1m4f and sound run when 2nd on AW latest, but best form on good or softer

runners.name: Something
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner for Stuart Williams (1m4f, AW/good); made a winning debut for Liam Bailey when dictating matters in a five-runner handicap at Ripon last time (1m4f, good; cheekpieces back on); headgear retained; up 4lb; contender.
runners.comment: Made winning debut for Liam Bailey when making all at Ripon last time; up 4lb; contender

runners.name: Raulin
runners.spotlight: Three wins, all over 1m2f, last season (good/good to firm; two here); best run this term when second of four at Hamilton last time (1m5f, good to firm); a possible.
runners.comment: Three wins over 1m2f, two here, in 2025; decent 2nd of four last time (1m5f); a possible

runners.name: Aulis
runners.spotlight: Won a Southwell handicap (1m4f; visor first time) and was placed seven times for Ralph Beckett in 2024; subsequently sold for 42,000gns in October 2024, but hasn't run since; no easy task after a long break.
runners.comment: AW winner for Ralph Beckett in 2024; bought 42,000gns; best watched after 600-day absence

races.race_name: Tony Bethell Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Rupert The Prince
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner at up to 2m under varying tactics; hasn't run at all badly in his two races this campaign and couldn't rule out.
runners.comment: Hasn't run at all badly in his two races this campaign and couldn't rule out

runners.name: Cold Henry
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles in March but hasn't offered a great deal since, in that discipline and back on the Flat; has become hard to predict.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles in March but hasn't offered a great deal since; hard to predict

runners.name: Charlie Darling
runners.spotlight: Won 1m6f/2m handicaps on yielding when trained in Ireland; added two more on the AW for this yard before an underwhelming run at Carlisle where perhaps the ground was too soft; he's another with each-way claims.
runners.comment: Four wins divided between AW/turf; perhaps found the ground too soft last time

runners.name: Angelardo
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to finish ahead of Lady Phoebe at Newcastle (2m) but he's inconsistent and has yet to prove himself on turf.
runners.comment: Kept on well to finish ahead of Lady Phoebe at Newcastle (2m) but he's inconsistent

runners.name: Someone's Wish
runners.spotlight: A fair winning hurdler/chaser for Gordon Elliott but signed off for that yard out form and was beaten a long way on yard debut on the AW (1m4f).
runners.comment: Capable in the past but beaten a long way on yard debut on the AW (1m4f)

runners.name: Simiyann
runners.spotlight: Two 2m wins last year on good and faster ground; a return to hurdling stalled his progress and hasn't been at his best back on the Flat in his last two races, though 1m4f was on the short side after a break at Newbury; could stage a revival.
runners.comment: 1m4f too sharp after a break and could stage a revival returned to this far

runners.name: Lady Phoebe
runners.spotlight: Won by 5.5l over the extended 2m5f here before getting outpaced in a sprint finish back over 2m at Newcastle; remains in good form and entitled to feature.
runners.comment: Won over 2m5f here before getting outpaced off slow fractions back over 2m on the AW

runners.name: Golden Samba
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and first run at 2m when beaten a shade under 6l at Lingfield (good), though could have been flattered from the front; others have more concrete claims.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and first run at 2m when beaten a shade under 6l at Lingfield

runners.name: Arcimboldo
runners.spotlight: Started off for Mike Sowersby with three good runs last autumn (all 2m, Tapeta) but his form then dipped and he's returning from a lengthy break; remains a maiden.
runners.comment: Maiden whose form has dipped and he's returning from a lengthy absence

runners.name: Laravie
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner who has won her last two Flat races, comfortably defying a penalty at the expense of Zimmerman at Catterick (2m, good); 6lb rise isn't the end of the world, especially in view of her hurdling ability.
runners.comment: Comfortably beat Zimmerman at Catterick and a 6lb rise isn't the end of the world

runners.name: Zimmerman
runners.spotlight: Should be well handicapped and has run well in defeat with cheekpieces added (joining regular tongue-tie) in last two races, behind Laravie in the penultimate one; surprising to see the blinkers back on.
runners.comment: Some return to form with cheekpieces added, so surprising to see blinkers back on

runners.name: Merrijig
runners.spotlight: Was 2-3 on the AW until disappointing at Kempton two months ago but he had been running well in the blinkers (reapplied) and he's a three-time turf winner on a competitive mark.
runners.comment: Had been running well in blinkers which were left off last time; fair mark

races.race_name: Bill Carrigill Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Seventy
runners.spotlight: Hard to knock in his last five races, from 1m2f to 1m4f; looked as though he'd win last time at Windsor before going down narrowly (three ran); same mark this evening.
runners.comment: Hard to knock in his last five races, from 1m2f to 1m4f; should be involved

runners.name: Off Spin
runners.spotlight: Possibilities on a few of his efforts, including the third over this trip at Ripon two runs back; didn't appear to stay 1m4f last time.
runners.comment: Probable non-stayer over 1m4f last time and was placed before then at Ripon
runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor

runners.name: Princess Niyla
runners.spotlight: Placed last time at Redcar (1m2f, good) and that was behind two 3yos; this might be more realistic and down 2lb.
runners.comment: Placed last time at Redcar (1m2f, good) and that was behind two 3yos

runners.name: Nanny Park
runners.spotlight: Won at two for James Fanshawe and had competitive form off higher marks last year; however, she's finished last in two 1m2f handicaps since moving stables.
runners.comment: She's finished last in two 1m2f handicaps since moving stables

runners.name: Arranmore
runners.spotlight: First and second in 1m3f handicaps at Southwell before unsuited by the drop back to 1m at Ripon; he's fine on turf and could be given another chance.
runners.comment: 1st and 2nd in 1m3f handicaps at Southwell before unsuited by the drop back to 1m at Ripon

runners.name: Falcon Nine
runners.spotlight: Only 2-33, yet those wins were last August over 1m1f on good ground; back on a useful mark and might be sharper for his recent run after a break.
runners.comment: Back on a useful mark and might be sharper for his recent run after a break

runners.name: Porter's Song
runners.spotlight: Only had four races and now on his third trainer after changing hands in May for 5,000gns; did finish fifth of 14 on his final run for Ed Bethell, so may have more to offer.
runners.comment: Did finish fifth of 14 on his final run for Ed Bethell, so may have more to offer

runners.name: Sweet Cicely
runners.spotlight: Has the odd bit of competitive form but last three runs have been very quiet and she's now 0-19.
runners.comment: Has the odd bit of competitive form but last three runs have been very quiet

runners.name: Lenny's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner; narrowly beaten on yard debut at Lingfield in March (1m2f AW) but this 8yo's form has cooled.
runners.comment: Form has cooled since going close on stable debut in March

runners.name: African Spirit
runners.spotlight: Has lost his way since looking promising initially and finished last on yard debut at Newmarket after a lengthy absence; that was over 7f, but might not be crying out for this far.
runners.comment: Has lost his way since looking promising initially; not certain to appreciate this far

runners.name: Satyress
runners.spotlight: Modest profile includes two defeats in 1m2f handicaps and she's been off since September.
runners.comment: Modest profile includes two defeats in 1m2f handicaps and she's been off since September

runners.name: Shimmering Sands
runners.spotlight: No wins since 2023 and although he was running well when last seen, that was late last year; probably one to watch unless backed.
runners.comment: Although he was running well when last seen, that was late last year

races.race_name: Mr Wolf Sprint Handicap

runners.name: Daytona Lady
runners.spotlight: Has won on turf (over 5.5f at Wetherby in June 2024), but races mainly on the AW these days; comes into the reckoning following a narrow defeat at Wolverhampton last time (6f); well drawn.
runners.comment: Turf winner, but races mainly on AW; comes into the reckoning after good AW 2nd last time
runners.owner: Eric Broadwith And Mrs Ruth Carr

runners.name: Call Me Betty
runners.spotlight: Seemed suited by the drop back to 6f when making all to win at Carlisle last time (good to soft; first turf win, second only fifth next time); up 2lb; a possible.
runners.comment: Suited by drop to 6f when making all at Carlisle last time; up 2lb; a possible

runners.name: Just Queen High
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Dylan Cunha, the second a Nottingham handicap (6f) on heavy ground last October when blinkered for the first time; poor efforts both starts this term; first run for Ian Williams.
runners.comment: Two wins for Dylan Cunha last term, second with blinkers; poor this term; stable debut

runners.name: Great
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner for Richard Hannon last May (6f, good to firm); yet to hit top form for new yard this season (excuses last time) including two runs over 7f; on a good mark and could go well from good draw.
runners.comment: Maiden winner for Richard Hannon; not at best so far in 2026; well treated now; chance

runners.name: Emperor's Son
runners.spotlight: Only turf win came at Carlisle on debut in June 2023 (5f, good to firm); won again at Wolverhampton in January (5f) but may just need this after 122-day break.
runners.comment: Only turf win came on debut in 2023; AW win in January (5f); back after a break
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Dorney Lake
runners.spotlight: Wasn't beaten far when eighth at Redcar last time (6f, good to firm) and having been dropped again, is now on a good mark; could go well with cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Fair run at Redcar latest; on a good mark now; could go well with cheekpieces tried
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Dicko The Legend
runners.spotlight: Both turf wins have been over 6f at York, the latest a 22-runner handicap last July (good) when back after wind surgery; creditable sixth at Redcar last time (6f, good to firm); a possible.
runners.comment: Won 22-runner h'cap at York last July; creditable sixth at Redcar last time; a possible
runners.owner: Mrs S Hibbert, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Fiscal Policy
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner, including over C&D in September 2024; in fair form without winning this season; wide draw not ideal; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Eight wins, including over C&D; in fair form without winning this season; each-way chance
runners.owner: S Thompson, Mrs R Carr & G Shields

runners.name: Veydari
runners.spotlight: Best form on soft ground, his only win coming over C&D in October 2024; makes her seasonal debut and would be interesting if the ground eases.
runners.comment: Best on soft ground (only win over C&D on soft in October); interesting if going eases

runners.name: Hectic
runners.spotlight: Ripon winner off a 3lb higher mark last June (6f, good); not in top form this season but should fare better on first run after having wind surgery
runners.comment: Ripon winner last June; not been in top form; should do better on first run after wind op
